title: Executive Order 9293—Quarry Heights Military Reservation, Canal Zone

flags.justification: Standard military-reservation land-designation template (Canal Zone), superseding a 1936 order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; retains Canal Zone civil-jurisdiction carve-out.

title: Executive Order 9294—Further Defining of the Functions and Duties of the Office of Defense Transportation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Director granted review/approval authority over Federal and private-firm passenger-transportation-equipment contracts 'as the Director considers necessary,' extending the already-scored ODT framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-limitation of WPB/ICC/War Shipping Administration authority (Section I.4); genuine disclosed common-carrier and military-operations exemptions (Sections I.4-5).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As the Director considers necessary' is a self-determined standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-limitation clauses preserve other agencies' authority.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow extension within the already-scored ODT framework (EO 8989/9156/9214), not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9295—Amending Executive Order No. 9206 of July 27, 1942, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Granting of Allowances for Quarters and Subsistence to Enlisted Men

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical wording clarification to the already-scored EO 9206 (adds 'not to exceed' ceiling language, a bounding clarification).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9206.

title: Executive Order 9297—Making Certain Changes in the Customs Field Organization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine administrative customs-port boundary adjustments.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9298—Amending Subdivisions VII and XVI of Schedule A of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical Civil Service Schedule amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9299—Prescribing Regulations and Procedure With Respect to Wage and Salary Adjustments for Employees Subject to the Railway Labor Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Chairman of the National Railway Labor Panel is granted authority to determine whether proposed railway wage/salary changes 'conform' to wartime stabilization standards, with power to trigger Emergency Board review, effectively serving as a wage-control gatekeeper for the entire railway industry.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of National Mediation Board/National Railway Labor Panel jurisdiction (Section 1 proviso); genuine disclosed mandatory distribution of Emergency Board reports to the Economic Stabilization Director, National War Labor Board, and IRS Commissioner (Section 5).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Reason to believe... may not conform' is a fairly open, self-determined trigger standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Operates within and implements the already-scored EO 9250/9172 stabilization framework; genuine disclosed 30-day automatic-effectiveness safety valve for Emergency Board recommendations absent Director objection.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the wartime wage-stabilization program's significance to railway labor relations specifically, a sector critical to war logistics.

title: Executive Order 9300—Establishing the Interdepartmental Committee to Consider Cases of Subversive Activity on the Part of Federal Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Committee holds an advisory/coordinating role over federal-employee 'subversive activity' investigations, and Section 10 permits it to recommend 'special action in exceptional cases' to the President that bypasses the normal FBI-investigation/agency-determination procedure established elsewhere in this same order, upon a self-determined finding that 'internal security will be endangered' by the normal process.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed five-member Committee (not a single official); genuine disclosed non-limitation of Civil Service Commission review authority and existing suspension/removal procedures (Section 11); genuine disclosed carve-out excluding Navy/War Department employees absent specific departmental request.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Internal security will be endangered' is a self-determined standard triggering the emergency-bypass mechanism.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Section 10 emergency-bypass mechanism is a genuine, disclosed preemption of the normal investigative procedure the order itself establishes — the order creates its own protective process, then also creates a self-determined exception to it.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as an early institutional precursor to the more expansive federal loyalty-security programs of the following decade.

title: Executive Order 9301—Establishing a Minimum Wartime Workweek of Forty-Eight Hours

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a nationwide, mandatory 48-hour minimum workweek standard for all war-production plants; the War Manpower Commission Chairman is granted broad self-determined authority to interpret and apply the order and to adjust the minimum 'greater or less' as he determines.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed non-supersession of the Fair Labor Standards Act, state/local law, and collective-bargaining agreements (Section 5) — a real, disclosed protective carve-out.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he determines to be necessary' is a broad, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 'For the duration of the war,' open-ended.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed FLSA/collective-bargaining preservation constrains the order's reach into wage-and-hour law generally.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as a major nationwide wartime labor-utilization mandate touching the entire war-production workforce.

title: Executive Order 9302—Transferring to the Commissioner of Internal Revenue Certain Functions Relating to Taxes and Penalties Imposed for Violations of the National Prohibition Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical historical-cleanup transfer (Prohibition-era tax/penalty administration, a decade after repeal) to an accountable existing official, implementing existing statutory compromise procedures.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope; genuine disclosed compliance requirement with Internal Revenue Code compromise procedures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9303—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical Foreign Service fee-schedule and procedural amendments.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9305—Transfer of Certain Personnel Among the Coast and Geodetic Survey and the War and Navy Departments

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel transfer matching the established Coast and Geodetic Survey inter-service-transfer template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individuals, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause; also returns two previously-transferred officers to civilian jurisdiction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9304—Designating the Honorable Martin Travieso as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named judicial-appointment matter implementing existing statutory authority, fixed to the current calendar year.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed statutory basis, bounded term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Bounded to the current calendar year.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9306—Possession Relinquished of Plants of Triumph Explosives, Inc., Located at Elkton, Maryland

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action relinquishing Navy possession of the Triumph Explosives plants seized under EO 9254, per the genuine disclosed termination standard already built into that order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

title: Executive Order 9308—Revoking the Designation of Gastonia, North Carolina, as a Customs Port of Entry in Customs Collection District Number 15 (North Carolina)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine, deregulatory administrative customs-port revocation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
