flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9307—Amending Executive Order No. 8384 of March 29, 1940, Prescribing Regulations Relating to Annual Leave of Government Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A genuinely protective amendment extending federal employees' annual-leave-accumulation benefits, implementing a specific December 1942 statutory amendment, with genuine disclosed retroactive-application protection for employees.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed numeric caps and retroactive-adjustment mechanism.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective of employee benefits; no oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9311—Enlarging the Squaw Creek National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard, small (374.64-acre) wildlife-refuge enlargement template (Missouri), subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9310—Transferring the Nutrition Functions of the Office of Defense Health and Welfare Services to the Department of Agriculture

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow inter-agency function transfer (nutrition functions) to an accountable existing Cabinet official, consistent with the already-scored EO 9280 food-program framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9309—Controlling Government Requests for the Selective Service Deferment of Federal Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The War Manpower Commission Chairman-supervised Review Committee holds binding authority over federal-agency deferment requests government-wide, including power to suspend any Agency Committee's authority and cancel improper deferment requests; 'key position' criteria are broadly self-determined.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-committee structure (a central Review Committee plus individual 3-5-member Agency Committees) — real distributed decision-making rather than single-official control; genuine disclosed appeal mechanism to the Review Committee for employees denied voluntary military service.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Key position' criteria include some fairly open standards (e.g., 'peculiar circumstances or requisite physical abilities') alongside more specific ones.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The overarching program is standing/tied to the war, though individual deferment requests are capped at six months (a genuine disclosed sub-bound).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed mandatory appeal process for employees denied voluntary enlistment; genuine disclosed periodic (quarterly) reporting-to-President requirement (Section VII.6); genuine disclosed Review Committee oversight of Agency Committees.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as the mechanism balancing federal civilian-workforce retention against escalating wartime military manpower needs.

title: Executive Order 9312—Defining the Foreign Information Activities of the Office of War Information

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, diff-aware amendment to the already-scored EO 9182 (Office of War Information, 31.25%) that SUBORDINATES rather than expands OWI's authority: foreign propaganda in military-operations areas is made 'subject to the approval of the Joint Chiefs of Staff' and theater-commander control — a check-adding clarification, not a new grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Joint Chiefs of Staff approval requirement and theater-commander control mechanism.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed military-command subordination constrains OWI's foreign-propaganda reach rather than expanding it.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow clarifying scope; the underlying OWI significance is already captured in EO 9182's independent score.

title: Executive Order 9313—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9231 of August 20, 1942, to the Construction of Red Mountain Chromite Road in the Territory of Alaska

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware geographic extension of the already-scored EO 9231 eight-hour-law suspension template to one additional named road project.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9231, including its overtime-pay protection.

title: Executive Order 9314—Revoking Executive Order No. 8955 of December 1, 1941, Extending the Classification Act of 1923, as Amended, to Certain Positions in the War Department and in the Navy Department and Establishing a Salary Differential Therefor

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion revoking a prior salary-differential order, upon Civil Service Commission recommendation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed basis (CSC recommendation).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores standard classification treatment rather than removing oversight.

title: Executive Order 9315—Transferring Certain Functions From the President to the Secretary of Agriculture

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow inter-agency function transfer (a specific 1936 statutory function) to an accountable existing Cabinet official, matching the established First War Powers Act reorganization-to-accountable-official pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9316—Amending Section 8 of Executive Order No. 8942 or November 19, 1941, Entitled "Providing for the Administration of the Requisitioning of Property Required for National Defense"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment reducing (not eliminating) an existing reporting requirement in the already-CRITICAL-scored EO 8942 from quarterly to semi-annual.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The reporting mechanism, though weakened, remains disclosed and in place.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Actively weakens (reduces the frequency of) a disclosed pre-existing oversight/reporting-to-President check, matching the 'quiet regression' pattern already established at EO 9138 and EO 9267 — though less severe than outright revocation since the check survives in reduced form.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, technical administrative matter.

title: Executive Order 9317—Making Available for the Use of the Army and Navy Quinine Sulfate Acquired by the Procurement Division of the Treasury Department Pursuant to the Act of June 7, 1939

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow commodity-disposition authorization (quinine sulfate stockpile) implementing existing 1939 statutory authority, matching the established Manila fiber/optical glass/block mica pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed narrow purpose and fixed quantities.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9319—Amending Executive Order No. 9276 Entitled "Establishing the Petroleum Administration for War and Defining its Functions and Duties"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical amendment delegating internal administrative flexibility (Deputy Administrator operational discretion) within the already-scored EO 9276 (43.75%); does not change the core institutional structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9276.

title: Executive Order 9320—Amending Executive Order No. 9108 of March 21, 1942, Directing the Director of the Office of Defense Transportation to Take Control of the Toledo, Peoria and Western Railroad Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment adding financial-advance authority to the already-scored EO 9108 (railroad seizure, 20%), with genuine disclosed protective restrictions (no executive-salary payments beyond necessary amounts, no dividends, repayment tied to final claim adjudication).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed restrictions constrain the advance authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed restrictions preserved.

title: Executive Order 9321—Authorizing the Attorney General To Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Attorney General to acquire, use, and dispose of property 'deemed necessary' for 'military, naval or other war purposes' under Second War Powers Act Title II, matching the broader (non-purpose-limited) treatment given EO 9129/9179/9186/9217.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed necessary' is a brief, self-determined standard with no further criteria disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism implementing a specific Congressional Title, not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9322—Centralizing and Delegating Authority With Respect to the Production and Distribution of Food

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware transfer of ALL of the extraordinarily broad, already-CRITICAL EO 9280 food-control authority (62.5%) to a new, single Presidentially-appointed Administrator 'directly responsible to' the President, while additionally consolidating the Commodity Credit Corporation and the Extension Service into the same office — a further concentration atop an already-critical authority base.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed institutional continuity: the Secretary of Agriculture continues as Chairman of the EO 9280 Inter-Departmental Advisory Committee, continues as a War Production Board member, and continues as the American representative on the Combined Food Board — real disclosed checks survive the transfer.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the already-extensively-bundled EO 9280 authority with the Commodity Credit Corporation (a major credit/financing institution) and the Extension Service (a large, historic agricultural-education institution) into a single office — an even more extensive bundle than EO 9280 itself.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9280's broad self-determined standards governing the underlying food-control powers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9280's disclosed dispute-escalation and non-limitation mechanisms preserving OPA and Economic Stabilization Director authority.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Inherits and continues EO 9280's already-critical historical significance as the operative wartime food-control framework, now under a dedicated full-time Administrator (this reorganization would itself be superseded within weeks by EO 9334, the War Food Administration).

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Consolidates the Food Production Administration, Food Distribution Administration, Commodity Credit Corporation, and Extension Service into a single new Administration — substantial inter-agency consolidation.

title: Executive Order 9324—Amending the Manual for Courts-Martial, United States Army (1928)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A large but purely technical batch of courts-martial procedural amendments (notarial authority, evidence/record-destruction procedures, form corrections); implements existing statutory military-justice framework with no new unchecked discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive, clear, disclosed technical detail throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; procedural housekeeping only.

title: Executive Order 9323—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8809 of June 28, 1941, Establishing the Good Conduct Medal

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical amendment extending Good Conduct Medal eligibility criteria (wartime one-year-service alternative to the standard three-year requirement).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed eligibility criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9327—Providing for the More Effective Handling of Governmental Problems in Congested Production Areas in Order To Further the Successful Prosecution of the War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Committee's 'policies and decisions... shall be controlling on all Federal agencies to which they apply' (Section 7) — binding, cross-agency directive authority; the Committee is also empowered to designate and terminate 'congested production area' status on its own self-determined judgment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-member (seven-agency) Committee structure (Bureau of the Budget Chairman plus War/Navy/WPB/Federal Works Agency/National Housing Agency/War Manpower Commission representatives), not a single official; genuine disclosed Area Advisory Councils incorporating State, local-government, and community representatives (Section 6).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As it may deem advisable' is a self-determined designation standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to First War Powers Act Title I duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-agency committee and advisory-council structure constrains unilateral action.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as the coordinating mechanism for severe wartime housing and infrastructure strain in boomtowns near major shipbuilding and aircraft-production hubs.

title: Executive Order 9326—Transect of Jurisdiction Over Certain Land Containing Oil and Gas Deposits From the War Department to the Department of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective jurisdictional transfer (California drainage protection) designed to safeguard the United States' financial interest in oil/gas deposits, with a genuine disclosed War Department primary-jurisdiction retention for flood-control and navigation purposes.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description and retained War Department carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed War Department jurisdiction carve-out preserved.

title: Executive Order 9325—Payment of Expenses of the Office of Alien Property Custodian

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical implementation order authorizing expense payment/allocation for the already-scored Alien Property Custodian (EO 9095/9193), notably ADDING a genuine disclosed Bureau of the Budget expense-approval check (Section 5) not previously present — a check-adding amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Bureau of the Budget approval requirement for administrative expenses beyond April 30, 1943.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed BoB oversight mechanism added, not removed.

title: Executive Order 9328—Stabilization of Wages, Prices, and Salaries

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The famous 'Hold the Line' Order: directs the Price Administrator and Food Administrator to freeze/reduce prices nationwide 'on all commodities affecting the cost of living,' directs wage-control agencies to authorize 'no further increase in wages or salaries' with narrow exceptions, and grants the Economic Stabilization Director sweeping authority to 'exercise all powers and duties conferred upon the President' under the Act of October 2, 1942 — an extraordinarily broad, simultaneous nationwide freeze on prices, wages, salaries, and utility rates.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed exceptions preserved: the 'Little Steel formula' cost-of-living wage catch-up mechanism is expressly preserved; genuine disclosed authority for 'reasonable adjustments' for promotions, reclassifications, merit increases, and incentive wages; genuine disclosed continuation of the existing EO 9250 framework and agencies (Section 6) rather than wholesale replacement.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles price control, wage control, salary control, and utility-rate control into a single sweeping stabilization directive spanning multiple previously-distinct regulatory domains (OPA, National War Labor Board, IRS Commissioner, state/local utility regulators).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Clearly necessary to correct substandards of living' and 'excessively high, unfair, or inequitable' are broad, self-determined standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the Act of October 2, 1942 and the EO 9250 framework, with no separate fixed expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of existing agency structures (National War Labor Board, IRS Commissioner, EO 9250 apparatus) and the 'Little Steel formula' catch-up mechanism constrain the freeze from being absolute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically landmark: this is the 'Hold the Line' Order, one of the most significant and famous wartime anti-inflation measures of FDR's presidency, fundamentally reshaping the entire national wage-price relationship for the balance of the war.

title: Executive Order 9330—Transfer of Certain Central Administrative Services of the Office for Emergency Management

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical inter-agency function transfer (fiscal/personnel-recruitment/classification functions) to accountable existing agencies (OPA, WPB, Civil Service Commission).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Civil Service Commission standard-setting and post-audit check (Section 3).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed CSC oversight mechanism preserved and strengthened.

title: Executive Order 9329—Designating the Chairman of the United States Maritime Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel appointment implementing existing statutory authority, no new authority granted.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in the underlying Merchant Marine Act framework.

title: Executive Order 9333—Amending Subdivision XXIX of Schedule A of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical Civil Service Schedule amendment (Inland Waterways Corporation positions), with genuine disclosed sunset.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset (war plus six months).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9335—Providing Additional Members of the War Production Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware amendment to the already-scored EO 9024 (War Production Board, 68.75%) adding three more member-agency representatives; broadens rather than narrows internal Board representation, a mild accountability-improving change.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed narrow amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9024.

title: Executive Order 9332—Establishing the Solid Fuels Administration for War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Administrator (Secretary of the Interior ex officio) is granted broad, self-determined authority to establish basic policies and issue binding operating directives to the entire U.S. solid-fuels (coal) industry — matching the EO 9276 Petroleum Administration for War template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: the Administrator's enforcement power operates 'subject to the direction of the Chairman of the War Production Board'; extensive genuine disclosed multi-agency non-preemption clause (Sections 10-11: ICC, Federal Power Commission, War/Navy secret-military-necessity carve-outs).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles production-estimation, distribution-recommendation, pricing consultation, rationing consultation, transportation-recommendation, and manpower-request functions into a single office, coherently organized around the single solid-fuels-industry subject.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As the Administrator deems necessary' is a self-determined standard used throughout.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war, with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The extensive, explicit multi-agency carve-out list (matching EO 9276's Section 9 treatment) is a genuine, disclosed preemption-of-oversight safeguard.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as the wartime coal-industry-control institution, notable given major 1943 coal-mining labor disputes occurring concurrently.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly abolishes the Office of Solid Fuels Coordinator for War and absorbs its functions.

title: Executive Order 9334—War Food Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Formally establishes the 'War Food Administration,' consolidating and continuing the already-CRITICAL EO 9280/9322 food-control authority (subpoena power, full national supply-chain control) under a War Food Administrator, and adds an explicit anti-challenge clause (Section 4) barring third parties from challenging War Food Administration action on the ground that it fell within the Secretary of Agriculture's jurisdiction rather than the Administrator's, or vice versa.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed institutional continuity: the Secretary of Agriculture continues as Inter-Departmental Committee chairman, War Production Board member, and Combined Food Board representative; the War Food Administrator becomes alternate Combined Food Board representative and a member of the advisory Inter-Departmental Committee.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Continues the same extensive bundle as EO 9322 (Food Production Administration, Food Distribution Administration, Commodity Credit Corporation, Extension Service) while also explicitly integrating the EO 9328 'Hold the Line' stabilization powers (Section 2 cross-reference) into the Administrator's authority.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Inherits EO 9280/9322's broad self-determined standards governing the underlying food-control powers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Section 4 anti-challenge clause is a genuine, disclosed, and unusually explicit preemption of a specific class of legal challenge (jurisdictional-technicality challenges between the Secretary and the Administrator) — narrower in scope than EO 9193's blanket anti-validity-review clause, but still a real foreclosure of third-party legal recourse.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Inherits and formalizes EO 9280/9322's landmark historical significance as the operative, now-permanent wartime food-control framework (the War Food Administration would remain the central wartime food-control institution for the remainder of the war).

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Formalizes and continues the consolidation of the Food Production Administration, Food Distribution Administration, Commodity Credit Corporation, and Extension Service into a single Administration.

title: Executive Order 9331—Medal for Merit

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow amendment to the already-scored EO 9286: streamlines the Medal for Merit Board membership (removing WPB Chairman/OCD Director, retaining State/War/Navy) and supersedes EO 9286.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed three-member Board structure retained.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9286.

title: Executive Order 9336—Authorizing Financing Arrangements to Facilitate the Prosecution of the War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Lend-Lease and War Shipping Administrators to enter guaranties, indemnification agreements, and other financing arrangements 'without regard to the provisions of law relating to the making, performance, amendment or modification of contracts' — a real, disclosed override of normal contract-law procedural safeguards, narrowly implementing the already-scored EO 9112 financing mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed public-inspection/data-maintenance requirement (Section 3), though tempered by the Administrators' own discretion over what is 'compatible with the public interest' to disclose.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The public-inspection requirement is itself self-limited by Administrator discretion over what counts as 'restricted, confidential, or secret' — a real but weak check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow financing-mechanism matter, implementing the already-scored EO 9112 framework.

title: Executive Order 9339—Transfer of Civil Air Patrol From the Office of Civilian Defense to the Department of War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Inter-agency function transfer to an accountable existing Cabinet official, with genuine disclosed sunset.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset (First War Powers Act Title I duration).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9338—Abolishing the Office of Defense Health and Welfare Services and Transferring Its Functions to the Federal Security Agency

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Inter-agency consolidation/transfer of health and welfare functions to an accountable existing agency.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9340—Possession and Operation of Coal Mines

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of the Interior is granted broad, self-determined, open-ended authority to seize ANY coal mine nationwide where a strike 'has occurred or is threatened' — not a single named facility but an entire-industry seizure trigger, with authority to 'do all things necessary for or incidental to' production, sale, and distribution.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed labor-protective provisions: workers' rights to union membership, collective bargaining, and grievance procedures are explicitly preserved (distinguishing this from a pure strikebreaking order); genuine disclosed requirement that management retain 'managerial functions to the maximum degree possible' — real but non-binding disclosed constraints.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary' and an anticipatory 'threatened' (not yet actual) strike trigger are broad, self-determined standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Open-ended, terminated only by the Secretary's own determination that operations are 'no longer required.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of collective-bargaining and grievance rights constrains the seizure from being a pure union-suppression tool.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: this seizure directly confronted the major 1943 United Mine Workers strikes led by John L. Lewis, a defining wartime labor-relations crisis with major political and economic consequences.

title: Executive Order 9343—Transferring Jurisdiction and Control Over Certain Lands on Government Island, California, to the Navy Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard defense-related land-jurisdiction transfer, tied to the proclaimed emergency, with genuine disclosed automatic reversion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed carve-outs preserving Public Roads Administration's continued building use and Coast Guard equipment-sharing arrangements.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reversion clause upon termination of the emergency.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9342—Amending Executive Order No. 8970 of December 11, 1941, Establishing Certain Defensive Sea Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical boundary redefinition of the already-scored Narragansett Bay Defensive Sea Area (EO 8970).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed boundary description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8970.

title: Executive Order 9344—Changing the Name of the Alley Dwelling Authority to National Capital Housing Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely nominal renaming; grants no new authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion created.

title: Executive Order 9347—Providing for the Establishment of an Office of War Mobilization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of War Mobilization is granted authority to 'unify the activities' of ALL federal agencies concerned with production, procurement, distribution, or transportation, to 'resolve and determine controversies between such agencies,' and to 'issue such directives on policy or operations to the Federal agencies and departments as may be necessary' — binding, cross-cutting super-agency authority sitting above WPB, WMC, WSA, and the entire war-mobilization apparatus.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: the Office operates 'subject to the direction and control of the President' and 'in consultation with' the War Mobilization Committee (Secretaries of War and Navy, Munitions Assignments Board Chairman, WPB Chairman, Economic Stabilization Director) — real multi-Cabinet-level consultative structure, though the Committee's role is advisory ('advise and consult') rather than a binding veto.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles resource allocation, manpower, economic-stabilization coordination, and inter-agency dispute resolution into a single apex office sitting above the entire war-mobilization apparatus (WPB, WMC, WSA, OPA, and others) — the most extensive coordinating scope encountered in this stream.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As may be necessary to carry out the programs developed' is a broad, self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed exception preserving the Director of Economic Stabilization's separate dispute-resolution authority under EO 9250 Title IV Section 3 (Section III(b) proviso) — a real, disclosed carve-out.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically landmark: the Office of War Mobilization became the most powerful civilian war-management institution of WWII, functioning as the operational center of the entire wartime economy, and would later evolve into the Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion overseeing postwar demobilization.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly sits above and can resolve disputes among WPB, WMC, WSA, and other agencies — the ultimate expression of the inter-agency-coordination/consolidation pattern seen throughout 1942-43.

title: Executive Order 9345—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, and Declared Value Excess-Profits Tax Returns by the Naval Affairs Investigating Committee, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants a congressional investigative committee broad access to tax returns spanning multiple years, for the stated purpose of carrying out a broadly-worded House Resolution investigative mandate, matching the treatment given the analogous EO 9281 (Un-American Activities Committee).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Treasury regulatory compliance requirement governing the inspection.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Treasury regulatory constraint preserved; an inter-branch (executive-to-legislative) information grant rather than an executive concentration of power.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as part of the wartime pattern of congressional investigative committees gaining broad tax-return access, paralleling the Un-American Activities Committee grant.

title: Executive Order 9346—Further Amending Executive Order No. 8802 by Establishing a New Committee on Fair Employment Practice and Defining its Powers and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The reconstituted Committee (up to seven members) is granted power to receive and investigate discrimination complaints, 'conduct hearings, make findings of fact, and take appropriate steps to obtain elimination of such discrimination' — real investigative authority, though notably without disclosed subpoena power or independent binding-sanction authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-member Committee structure (not a single official); genuine disclosed structural enforcement mechanism requiring non-discrimination clauses in all government contracts and subcontracts, rather than direct executive-branch penalty power.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Discrimination... because of race, creed, color, or national origin' is a reasonably specific, disclosed standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Committee's power is investigative/recommendatory rather than adjudicative-with-independent-sanctions — a real, disclosed limitation on its enforcement reach beyond the contract-compliance mechanism.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically landmark: the wartime Fair Employment Practice Committee is a foundational precursor to the postwar civil-rights-era equal-employment framework, addressing racial discrimination in war-industry employment in the wake of the 1943 Detroit race riot.

title: Executive Order 9348—Amending Section 9 of Executive Order No. 8588 Entitled "Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of Household Goods and Personal Effects of Certain Civilian Officers and Employees of the United States"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical, protective amendment accommodating wartime food-rationing realities within existing household-goods transportation regulations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 8588.

title: Executive Order 9349—Possession Relinquished of Plants of Milford Ordnance Company, Inc., and Sussex Ordnance Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action relinquishing Navy possession of the Milford/Sussex Ordnance plants seized under EO 9254, per the genuine disclosed termination standard already built into that order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

title: Executive Order 9350—Conferring Certain Additional Authority Upon the Administrator of the War Shipping Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends the already-CRITICAL WSA Administrator's authority (EO 9054) to acquire/requisition FOREIGN merchant vessels within U.S. jurisdiction, with a retroactive ratification clause (Section 2) validating actions taken since June 30, 1942 — before this order's own issuance — a mild disclosed accountability concern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed sunset (six months after war termination).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The retroactive-ratification clause validates past unilateral action after the fact, a real (if narrow) disclosed accountability gap.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow extension within the already-scored WSA framework.

title: Executive Order 9352—Entry of Alien Seamen Into the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but well-bounded immigration/security procedural framework, implementing existing statutory authority with detailed, disclosed exemption categories and waiver procedures.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural detail throughout, including disclosed Secretary of State/Attorney General joint-regulation-making authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; a detailed, disclosed regulatory framework.

title: Executive Order 9353—Disposal of Electric Energy Generated at the Norfork Project

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Federal Works Administrator is granted self-determined authority to allocate transmission/distribution facilities 'to such extent as, in the judgment of the Administrator, will not substantially interfere with the other uses' — self-determined allocation authority reaching privately-owned infrastructure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Federal Power Commission rate-approval requirement (a real independent-agency check); genuine disclosed preference-order for power sales (war plants, then public bodies/cooperatives, then others); genuine disclosed FPC fallback for facility-use-term disputes.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In the judgment of the Administrator' is a self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to First War Powers Act/Second War Powers Act Title III duration (dual sunset structure).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed FPC rate-approval and dispute-resolution fallback constrain Administrator discretion.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, regional infrastructure-coordination matter, not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9354—Designating the Chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission as Member of the Economic Stabilization Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware amendment broadening the already-scored EO 9250 Economic Stabilization Board's membership; a mild accountability-neutral addition.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9250.

title: Executive Order 9355—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9226 of August 19, 1942, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Men of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical, protective amendment adding a proviso preserving clothing-allowance entitlements for personnel who fail to complete officer training.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9226.

title: Executive Order 9356—Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely technical, tabular consolidation/rewrite of the clothing-allowance regulatory family (EO 9226/9283/9355), implementing the Pay Readjustment Act of 1942 with detailed, disclosed eligibility rules.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural detail throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; purely implements statutory pay formula.

title: Executive Order 9357—Transferring the Functions of the Public Works Administration to the Federal Works Administrator

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Inter-agency function transfer to an accountable existing official, with genuine disclosed preservation of existing-law personnel-separation procedures for excess staff.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuine disclosed 'existing law' personnel protections preserved.

title: Executive Order 9358—Providing for the Functions of the Board of Legal Examiners

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, explicitly interim/contingent measure ('pending action by the Congress... and until further order') transferring Board of Legal Examiners functions to the Civil Service Commission, an oversight body itself.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed interim character and continued applicability of the underlying EO 8743/9230 procedural framework.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicitly contingent/interim ('until further order' and pending congressional action), though without a fixed date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; vests authority in the CSC oversight body itself.

title: Executive Order 9359—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Suspends unspecified statutory employment provisions in three named 1944 appropriation acts for the Canal Zone, based on a self-determined 'public interest' standard; the underlying statutory text is not reproduced, limiting independent assessment of its full scope — matching the established EO 9188/9189 treatment.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to 'the continuance of any of the wars in which the United States is now engaged,' with no independently fixed expiration.

title: Executive Order 9360—Suspension of Eight-hour Law as to Laborers and Mechanics Employed by the Department of the Interior on Public Works Within the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical eight-hour-law suspension matching the established EO 9231/9251 template, with a genuine disclosed worker-protective offset (mandatory 1.5x overtime pay).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope and mandatory overtime-pay proviso.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the duration of the proclaimed emergencies, with no independently fixed expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed overtime-pay protection preserved.

title: Executive Order 9361—Supplementing the Executive Order Establishing the Office of War Mobilization and Providing for the Unifying of Foreign Economic Affairs

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the new Office of Economic Warfare is granted broad authority over all foreign economic-warfare activities plus direct control of four major government corporations (U.S. Commercial Company, Rubber Development Corporation, Petroleum Reserve Corporation, Export-Import Bank), including power to 'reconstitute the Boards of Directors of these corporations and take such other action as he deems necessary.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: Section 1's explicit spending restriction requires that certain foreign expenditures after August 15, 1943 be 'formally approved in writing by a majority of the War Mobilization Committee' and filed with the Secretary of State — a real, disclosed multi-party approval gate, notably instituted as a direct response to the preceding public controversy (the Wallace-Jones feud) over Board of Economic Warfare spending practices; genuine disclosed subordination to the newly-created Office of War Mobilization for cross-agency coordination.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles economic-warfare policy with four major government corporations (commercial trading, rubber development, petroleum reserves, and an international development bank) plus RFC foreign-financing authority into a single office.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary' is a self-determined standard for corporate-governance actions.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The genuine disclosed War Mobilization Committee spending-approval gate and State Department foreign-policy filing requirement constitute a real, disclosed check — notably stronger than most 1943 orders, given this was an explicit response to a public accountability controversy.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant: this reorganization arose directly from the highly public 1943 Wallace-Jones feud over Board of Economic Warfare spending and procurement practices, a major wartime governance controversy; the Office of Economic Warfare and its Foreign Economic Administration successor would remain central to wartime foreign economic policy.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Abolishes the Board of Economic Warfare and consolidates four separate government corporations plus RFC's foreign-economic functions into a single office.

title: Executive Order 9362—Transferring Certain Land From the Jurisdiction of the Federal Communications Commission to the Jurisdiction of the Navy Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard inter-agency land-jurisdiction transfer (Hawaii radio station site) for naval purposes.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9363—Redistributing Certain Functions of the Secretary of War and the Judge Advocate General With Respect to Certain Court-Martial Cases

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, internal delegation of court-martial review functions to subordinate officials, with genuine disclosed preservation of the original officials' discretion to act personally (Section 3 proviso).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed preservation of original-official override discretion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed override-discretion preservation constrains the delegation from being exclusive.

title: Executive Order 9364—Prescribing the Strength of the Women's Army Corps

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, one-sentence order implementing a specific contemporaneous congressional Act (July 1, 1943, establishing the fully-militarized WAC), with a genuine disclosed numeric cap (200,000).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis and cap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The numeric cap remains a real, disclosed bound.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion; implements explicit statutory authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as the order operationalizing the transition of women's Army service to full military status (militarization itself derives from the underlying Act, replacing the WAAC's 'auxiliary' status).

title: Executive Order 9365—Women's Army Corps Service Medal

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard military-decoration-eligibility template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed eligibility criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9366—Relating to the Operation and Disposition of Electric Energy at the Denison Dam, the Grand River Dam, and the Norfork Dam, in the States of Texas, Oklahoma, and Arkansas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of the Interior is granted self-determined allocation authority over transmission/distribution facilities 'to such extent as, in the judgment of the Secretary of the Interior, will not substantially interfere with the other uses' — matching the EO 9353 Norfork Project template but now consolidating THREE major dam projects (Denison, Grand River, Norfork) under unified control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Federal Power Commission rate-approval requirement; genuine disclosed preference-order for power sales; genuine disclosed FPC fallback for facility-use-term disputes.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In the judgment of the Secretary of the Interior' is a self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to First War Powers Act/Second War Powers Act Titles II-III duration (multi-Act sunset structure).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed FPC rate-approval and dispute-resolution fallback constrain discretion.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as part of the wartime hydroelectric-power-coordination apparatus feeding war industries across three states, broader than the single-project EO 9353.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Transfers dam-project authority away from the Federal Works Administrator to the Secretary of the Interior, consolidating multi-project dam administration.

title: Executive Order 9367—Prohibiting, With Certain Exceptions, Instruction of Applicants for Civil Service and Foreign Service Examinations by Officers or Employees of the Government

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A genuinely protective, anti-favoritism measure prohibiting government employees from privately coaching civil-service/Foreign Service examination applicants, with genuine disclosed exceptions for official veteran-training programs.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed exceptions and disciplinary consequence (removal from service).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective of merit-system integrity; no oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9368—Suspension of the Eight-Hour Law as to Laborers and Mechanics Employed by the Department of the Interior on Public Works Within the Territory of Alaska

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware geographic extension of the already-scored EO 9360 eight-hour-law suspension to Alaska public works, subject to the same conditions and limitations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9360, including its overtime-pay protection.

title: Executive Order 9370—Authorizing the Economic Stabilization Director To Take Certain Action in Connection With the Enforcement of Directives of the National War Labor Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director is granted a broad, multi-target enforcement mechanism reaching employers (withholding government priorities/benefits/contracts), unions (withholding accrued benefits/rights from non-complying labor organizations), and individuals (directing the War Manpower Commission to modify or cancel draft deferments for non-complying persons) — a uniquely coercive, cross-domain enforcement tool.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed trigger requirement: action follows only the National War Labor Board's own report of non-compliance, not self-initiated Director action; genuine disclosed escrow protection for union dues even when the check-off is denied — a real, disclosed protective mechanism for union funds.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary' is a self-determined standard governing the specific directives issued.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed NWLB-report trigger requirement constrains unilateral Director action; genuine disclosed dues-escrow protection.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given the increasingly severe wartime labor-enforcement apparatus, especially the individual-level draft-deferment-cancellation sanction — an unusually coercive tool tying employment compliance to conscription risk.

title: Executive Order 9369—Providing for the Liquidation of the Affairs of the Office of the Bituminous Coal Consumers' Counsel

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, administrative wind-down/liquidation matter for an expiring statutory office.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; a housekeeping liquidation matter.

title: Executive Order 9371—Amendment of Section 9 of Executive Order No. 8384 of March 29, 1940, Prescribing Regulations Relating to Annual Leave of Government Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A genuinely protective amendment providing furlough/unpaid-leave protection for employees whose positions are eliminated by statutory expiration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed 12-month cap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed 12-month cap.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuinely protective; no oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9372—Excepting Certain Persons From the Classification of "Alien Enemy" for the Purposes of Permitting Them To Apply for Naturalization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific, disclosed statutory delegation (Nationality Act Section 326(d)) allowing loyalty-based exceptions to alien-enemy classification, with a genuine disclosed Justice Department investigation/certification requirement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed investigation-and-certification requirement grounding the exception.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements explicit statutory authorization with disclosed investigative process.

title: Executive Order 9373—Operation of, and Disposition of Electric Energy at, the Denison Dam, the Grand River Dam, and the Norfork Dam in the States of Texas, Oklahoma, and Arkansas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware consolidation formalizing the same self-determined dam-facilities-allocation authority already scored across EO 9353/9366, now granting the Secretary of the Interior authority to 'integrate the power facilities' of three dam projects and exercise powers 'as in his judgment the public interest may require.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Federal Power Commission rate-approval requirement preserved.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In his judgment the public interest may require' is a self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to First War Powers Act/Second War Powers Act Titles II-III duration (multi-Act sunset structure).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed FPC check constrains discretion.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as the final consolidation of the wartime multi-dam hydroelectric coordination framework.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Explicitly revokes EO 9366 Part II and consolidates authority previously split across the Federal Works Administrator and the Secretary of the Interior into a single official.

title: Executive Order 9374—Transfer of Certain Personnel Between the Coast and Geodetic Survey and the Navy Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel transfer matching the established Coast and Geodetic Survey inter-service-transfer template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individuals, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause; also returns a previously-transferred officer to civilian jurisdiction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9375—Authorizing the War Shipping Administration To Take Possession of and Operate the Plant of the Atlantic Basin Iron Works, Incorporated, at Brooklyn, New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established plant-seizure template, now grounded in the newly-enacted War Labor Disputes Act (Smith-Connally Act) rather than inherent executive authority — a genuine disclosed new congressional-statutory basis providing Congressionally-defined procedural bounds not present in earlier inherent-authority seizures.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed civilian industrial-relations-advisor requirement; genuine disclosed express implementation of a specific National War Labor Board directive order (Aug. 25, 1943) — a real, disclosed institutional-process antecedent.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound termination trigger tied to restoration of 'productive efficiency' — more specific than the open-ended standard used in most seizures, though the seizure itself remains open-ended until that determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed War Labor Board-directive antecedent and Smith-Connally Act statutory grounding constrain the seizure to an adjudicated labor dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, single-plant matter within the broader wartime seizure pattern already extensively scored.

title: Executive Order 9376—Reinstating John W. Bailey, Junior, in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel reinstatement implementing existing statutory authority, subject to Senate confirmation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed statutory basis, Senate-confirmation contingency.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Senate-confirmation requirement preserved.

title: Executive Order 9377—Relinquishing Possession of the Plant of the Atlantic Basin Iron Works, Incorporated, at Brooklyn, New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action relinquishing WSA possession of the Atlantic Basin Iron Works plant seized under EO 9375, per the genuine disclosed termination standard already built into that order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

title: Executive Order 9378—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9062 of February 16, 1942, Authorizing the Civil Service Commission To Adopt Special Procedures Relating to Recruitment, Placement, and Changes in Status of Personnel for the Federal Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, diff-aware amendment to the already-scored EO 9062/9063; expands CSC's binding-procedure authority modestly while retaining the postmaster carve-out, recommended by the CSC oversight body itself.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope, recommended by CSC.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9062/9063.

title: Executive Order 9380—Foreign Economic Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Administrator is granted authority over ALL foreign economic affairs, absorbing the Office of Lend-Lease Administration, the Office of Foreign Relief and Rehabilitation Operations, the already-CRITICAL Office of Economic Warfare (with its four embedded government corporations), and the Office of Foreign Economic Coordination — a further concentration atop already-critical authority bases, with self-determined authority to reorganize sub-offices 'as he may deem desirable.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed check: powers are 'exercised in conformity with the foreign policy of the United States as defined by the Secretary of State' — a real, disclosed State Department subordination; genuine disclosed retention of the EO 9361 War Mobilization Committee spending-approval gate (Section 6).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles four major predecessor institutions (Lend-Lease, Foreign Relief and Rehabilitation Operations, Economic Warfare with its embedded corporations, Foreign Economic Coordination) into a single Administration — the most extensive foreign-economic-affairs bundle encountered in this stream.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem desirable in the interest of efficient administration' is a self-determined standard.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no separate expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed State Department foreign-policy subordination and War Mobilization Committee spending gate constitute real, disclosed checks.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: the Foreign Economic Administration became the central wartime and immediate-postwar institution for all foreign economic affairs, including liberated-areas supply administration — a landmark consolidation.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Abolishes/absorbs four separate predecessor institutions into a single Administration — the most extensive inter-agency consolidation event encountered in the stream to date.

title: Executive Order 9381—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9250, Entitled "Providing for the Stabilizing of the National Economy"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, diff-aware amendment to the already-scored EO 9250, notably ADDING a genuine disclosed $5,000 wage-decrease floor (a worker-protective addition) while also granting the National War Labor Board blanket exemption authority for small wage increases (a flexibility-adding, not power-concentrating, change).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope and protective floor.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9250; genuinely protective floor added.

title: Executive Order 9382—Making Certain Changes in the Customs Field Organization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine administrative customs-port boundary changes and revocation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9384—Submission of Reports to Facilitate Budgeting Activities of the Federal Government

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Bureau of the Budget Director is granted 'controlling' determination authority over the scope and application of a government-wide public-works advance-planning and reporting requirement, and departments must submit their Congressional reports on advance programs to the Bureau for 'advice' before submission — a real, disclosed pre-clearance mechanism over agency-Congress communications on public-works matters.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed transparency mechanism: when a report is submitted to Congress, it must include 'a statement of the advice received from the Bureau' (Section 4) — the pre-clearance influence cannot be exercised silently.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary' is a self-determined standard for reporting requirements.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed disclosure requirement prevents the pre-clearance mechanism from being covert.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as an early instance of formalized executive-branch pre-clearance over agency communications with Congress regarding public-works planning.

title: Executive Order 9383—Coordination of Functions and Policies of Federal Civil Agencies in Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, coordinative order granting the Secretary of the Interior only non-binding recommendation authority ('may make such recommendations') over other Federal civil agencies' Puerto Rico/Virgin Islands activities, with genuine disclosed carve-outs for judicial officers.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reporting-to-President-and-Congress requirement (Section 3).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Non-binding recommendation power only; no pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9385—Foreign Food Procurement and Development

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Transfers War Food Administration/Commodity Credit Corporation foreign food-procurement functions to the Foreign Economic Administration, but FEA's procurement must be performed 'consistently with directives issued... by the War Food Administrator' — a real, disclosed subordination tempering FEA's independent authority in this domain.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-directional consultation structure (WFA/WPB directives to FEA, FEA advisory input back to WFA/WPB/Office of War Mobilization) constitutes real, disclosed accountability.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed carve-outs preserving WFA/WPB's existing priorities and allocations authority (Section 4(b)).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, technical inter-agency coordination mechanism implementing the already-scored EO 9280/9380 frameworks.

title: Executive Order 9386—Regulations Governing the Granting of Allowances for Quarters and Subsistence to Enlisted Men

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely technical, tabular consolidation/rewrite of the already-scored EO 9206/9295 quarters-and-subsistence-allowance regulations, implementing the Pay Readjustment Act of 1942.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural detail throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; purely implements statutory pay formula.

title: Executive Order 9388—Establishing a Special Emergency Board To Report on Claims for Wage Adjustments of Nonoperating Railway Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, dispute-specific implementation of the already-scored EO 9172/9299 National Railway Labor Panel framework, establishing a three-member board for one specific wage dispute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-party reporting requirement (Economic Stabilization Director, National War Labor Board, Commissioner of Internal Revenue).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed reporting deadline (November 15, 1943) and 15-day effectiveness timeline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9172/9299.

title: Executive Order 9389—Amendment of Certain Paragraphs of the Executive Orders Establishing the Offices of Scientific Research and Development, Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs, Defense Transportation, and Civilian Defense

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely internal-administration amendment (delegating internal organization/committee-appointment authority) to four already-scored orders; grants no new substantive power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed narrow scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in the four amended orders.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard inter-agency land-transfer template (Agriculture to TVA) implementing the TVA Act, with genuine disclosed rights-of-way preservation for state/county roads.

flags.justification: Clear, disclosed metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9391—Transferring the Use, Possession, and Control of Certain Lands in the Cherokee National Forest From the Department of Agriculture to the Tennessee Valley Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard inter-agency land-transfer template (Agriculture to TVA) implementing the TVA Act, with an unusually detailed and genuinely protective set of disclosed Forest Service access/activity-rights reservations (Section listing three specific preserved rights).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed Forest Service rights preservation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed Forest Service rights preserved rather than removed.

title: Executive Order 9392—Amendment of Executive Order 9334 Entitled "War Food Administration"

flags.justification: A narrow, diff-aware clarifying amendment to the already-CRITICAL EO 9334, formalizing Combined Food Board membership and War Production Board membership continuity; does not expand the Administrator's core substantive authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9334.

flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9395-B—Possession of Leather Manufacturing Plants Located in Salem, Peabody, and Danvers, Massachusetts

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Note: the local corpus text stored under EO-9395 corresponds to a differently-numbered, unrelated order (a Marine Corps messmen-compensation regulation dated November 8, 1943) — a corpus/DB numbering mismatch of the same suffix type previously encountered with EO 9132-A, 9153-A, and 9242-A. This record is coded by title-pattern-matching: the disclosed title indicates a thirteen-plant leather-manufacturing seizure, confirmed by the companion relinquishment order (EO 9403, December 13, 1943), matching the established standard plant-seizure template (War Labor Disputes Act grounded, civilian industrial-relations-advisor requirement, open-ended termination pending Presidential/Secretarial determination) already scored at this level throughout the stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named plants, disclosed statutory basis (War Labor Disputes Act) inferred from the companion relinquishment order's citation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Open-ended pending determination, matching the standard seizure template.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Matches the standard seizure template's disclosed process constraints.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, multi-plant matter within the broader wartime seizure pattern already extensively scored.

title: Executive Order 9397—Numbering System for Federal Accounts Relating to Individual Persons

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Directs that all Federal agencies establishing new person-identification account-number systems must 'utilize exclusively' the Social Security Act account numbers — a broad standardization mandate reaching all future federal person-identification systems government-wide.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed confidentiality requirement (Section 4) and reimbursement/cost-transfer mechanism (Section 5) providing real institutional accountability for the service.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed confidentiality mandate constrains the universal-identifier system from being an open, unrestricted surveillance tool.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically extremely significant: this is the origin point of the now-ubiquitous use of the Social Security Number as a de facto universal personal identifier across the federal government, a foundational and enduring architecture with major long-term privacy and data-linkage implications that would be debated for decades afterward.

title: Executive Order 9396—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Issue Citations in the Name of the President of the United States to Army Units for Outstanding Performance in Action

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard unit-citation-eligibility template, superseding EO 9075 on the same matter.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.
