flags.justification: A genuinely protective, standard periodic extension of federal trust protections for Indian lands, matching the established EO 9272 template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope and fixed 25-year extension; genuine disclosed carve-out preserving Congress's own reserved authority.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed 25-year extension term.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuinely protective of beneficiaries' land rights.

title: Executive Order 9399—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy To Take Possession of and Operate Part of the Plant and Facilities of Remington Rand, Inc., in the Town of Southport, County of Chemung, State of New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established plant-seizure template, with a genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound termination trigger tied to Presidential determination of restored private operation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed requirement to employ 'a competent civilian adviser on industrial relations.'

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 60-day outer bound after determination, a somewhat more specific standard than the fully open-ended earlier seizure template.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No disclosed labor-board-directive antecedent narrative; narrow, single-division scope constrains it.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, single-facility matter within the broader wartime seizure pattern already extensively scored.

title: Executive Order 9400—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy To Take Possession of and Operate the Shipyard of the Los Angeles Shipbuilding and Drydock Corporation at Los Angeles, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established plant-seizure template, notably triggered by CONTRACTOR PERFORMANCE FAILURE rather than a labor dispute — a different, though structurally similar, seizure trigger; genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound termination.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed requirement to employ 'a competent civilian adviser on industrial relations.'

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 60-day outer bound after determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Narrow, single-facility scope.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, single-facility matter, notable as a rare non-labor-dispute seizure trigger but not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9402—Designating Dr. R.R. Sayers, Director of the Bureau of Mines, To Act as Under Secretary, First Assistant Secretary, or Assistant Secretary of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel-succession designation implementing existing statutory authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond existing succession law.

title: Executive Order 9401—Suspension of Eight-Hour Law as to Laborers and Mechanics Employed by the Department of Agriculture, Including the War Food Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical eight-hour-law suspension matching the established EO 9231/9251/9360 template, with a genuine disclosed worker-protective offset (mandatory 1.5x overtime pay).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope and mandatory overtime-pay proviso.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the duration of the proclaimed emergencies, with no independently fixed expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed overtime-pay protection preserved.

title: Executive Order 9403—Relinquishing Possession of Leather Manufacturing Plants Located in Salem, Peabody, and Danvers, Massachusetts

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A deregulatory reversion action relinquishing War Department possession of the thirteen leather-manufacturing plants seized under EO 9395-B, with a genuine disclosed proviso preserving the Secretary's ability to require post-seizure operational information for orderly wind-down.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed factual basis for the reversion determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Restores rather than removes oversight/ownership.

title: Executive Order 9407—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical Foreign Service fee-schedule amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in the amended regulation.

title: Executive Order 9405—Amending Subdivision IX of Schedule A of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical Civil Service Schedule amendment (Farm Credit Administration positions).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9404—Authorizing the Governor of the Panama Canal to Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Authorizes the Governor of the Panama Canal to acquire, use, and dispose of property 'deemed necessary' for 'military, naval or other war purposes' under Second War Powers Act Title II, matching the broader (non-purpose-limited) treatment given EO 9129/9179/9186/9217/9321.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Deemed necessary' is a brief, self-determined standard with no further criteria disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, tied to the war (Second War Powers Act duration), with no expiration disclosed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, procedural property-acquisition mechanism implementing a specific Congressional Title, not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9406—Transfer of Functions With Respect to Necessity Certificates From the Secretary of War and the Secretary of the Navy to the Chairman of the War Production Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The War Production Board Chairman is granted authority to certify tax-amortization-facilitating 'Necessity Certificates' for private war-production facility construction, consolidating authority previously split between the War and Navy Secretaries.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed two-part substantive test (Section 3(b): facilities must be both 'clearly necessary for the war effort' AND privately-financed to the Government's advantage) constrains certificate issuance; genuine disclosed consultation requirement with War/Navy Departments for military-use facilities (Section 4); genuine disclosed detailed transition/grandfathering rules (Section 2).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The two-part disclosed substantive test is more specific than most self-determined 'deemed necessary' standards, requiring an affirmative disclosed finding on both prongs.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed consultation requirements and the specific substantive test constrain unilateral WPB action.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A narrow, technical tax-certification consolidation matter, not independently landmark.

title: Executive Order 9408—Authorizing the Secretary of War To Take Possession of and Operate the Point Breeze Plants and Facilities of the Western Electric Company, Incorporated, in and Near the City of Baltimore, Maryland

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established plant-seizure template, PLUS explicitly invokes the EO 9370 individual-sanction mechanism, requesting the National War Labor Board withhold union benefits from the Point Breeze Employees Association and recommending draft-deferment cancellation against non-complying employees — a real escalation combining plant seizure with individual conscription-based coercion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed National War Labor Board referral requirement (a real check requiring an independent body's action before the union-benefit-withholding sanction takes effect).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound tied to 'productive efficiency' restoration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed NWLB referral requirement constrains unilateral Secretary action on the union-sanction piece.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as an escalating instance of the seizure-plus-individual-conscription-sanction combination first introduced via EO 9370.

title: Executive Order 9410—Delegation of Certain Functions of the President to the Director of Selective Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of Selective Service is granted delegated Presidential functions under the Selective Training and Service Act, now heading the Selective Service System as a SEPARATE agency independent of the War Manpower Commission (reversing its prior WMC-subordinate structure under EO 9279).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed mandatory War Manpower Commission consultation requirement (Section 3) preserves real coordination despite the institutional independence.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed WMC consultation requirement constrains independent action.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable given Selective Service's centrality to wartime manpower policy; this order's structural independence from WMC represents a meaningful institutional choice with lasting significance for the postwar Selective Service System.

title: Executive Order 9409—Altering the Composition of the War Manpower Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, diff-aware amendment broadening the already-scored EO 9139 War Manpower Commission's membership; does not change core substantive authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9139.

title: Executive Order 9411—Prescribing Rates for Hospitalization and Medical Care of Dependents of Naval Personnel and Others

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical rate-setting implementing a specific May 1943 statutory formula.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed rate schedule.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; purely implements statutory rate formula.

title: Executive Order 9412—Possession and Operation of Railroads

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of War is granted authority to seize possession and control of ALL common carriers by railroad NATIONWIDE (with disclosed narrow carve-outs for streetcars/interurbans, though even those may later be seized at the Secretary's own discretion), with broad 'do all things necessary' operational authority — an industry-wide, not single-facility, seizure trigger.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed labor-protective provisions (collective bargaining and union-membership rights preserved, Section 6); genuine disclosed preservation of existing contracts, dividends, and normal corporate operations (Section 5); genuine disclosed compliance-with-existing-law requirement (Section 7: ICC Act, Railway Labor Act, Safety Appliance Acts preserved).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary' is a self-determined standard; the strike trigger is anticipatory ('threatened,' not yet actual).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Open-ended, terminated only by the Secretary's own determination that possession is 'no longer required.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Section 8 explicitly bars institution of receivership, reorganization, or similar proceedings, and bars attachment, garnishment, or execution against seized carriers' property, without the Secretary's prior written consent — a broad, disclosed preemption of normal judicial and creditor oversight remedies during the open-ended seizure period.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically very significant: this was a nationwide railroad seizure preventing a strike that would have crippled wartime troop and supply movement during a critical period, one of the most consequential domestic wartime interventions of the entire war, paralleling the coal-mine seizure (EO 9340) as a defining instance of wartime industrial control — and, unlike most 1943-44 seizures, grounded in the older 1916 Army Appropriation Act authority rather than the War Labor Disputes Act.

title: Executive Order 9413—Establishing a Special Emergency Board to Report on the Claims for Wage Adjustments of Non-Operating Railway Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, dispute-specific implementation of the already-scored EO 9172/9299/9388 National Railway Labor Panel framework, addressing an unresolved sub-claim from the same underlying dispute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-party reporting requirement (Economic Stabilization Director, National War Labor Board, Commissioner of Internal Revenue).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed 30-day reporting deadline and 15-day effectiveness timeline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9172/9299/9388.

title: Executive Order 9414—Regulations Relating to Annual and Sick Leave of Government Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely technical, protective consolidation/rewrite of federal employee annual- and sick-leave regulations, superseding four prior orders, implementing existing statutory leave entitlements with detailed, disclosed accrual and administration rules.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive genuine disclosed procedural detail throughout, including disclosed CSC oversight of agency leave-account administration (Section 5.1).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; genuinely protective employee-benefit regulation.

title: Executive Order 9415—Transfer of Certain Personnel From the Coast and Geodetic Survey to the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, named personnel transfer matching the established Coast and Geodetic Survey inter-service-transfer template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individuals, disclosed statutory basis.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed automatic-reversion clause.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9416—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants of York Safe & Lock Company, York County, Pennsylvania

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the established plant-seizure template, with a genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound termination trigger.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed requirement to employ 'a competent civilian adviser on industrial relations.'

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: 60-day outer bound after determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Narrow, single-facility scope.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Narrow, single-facility matter within the broader wartime seizure pattern already extensively scored.

title: Executive Order 9417—Establishing a War Refugee Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Board (Secretaries of State, Treasury, and War) is charged with developing and executing rescue and relief plans with a broad, self-determined 'without limitation' functional scope; the Executive Director is granted operational authority to 'arrange for the prompt execution' of Board plans.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed three-Cabinet-Secretary Board structure, not a single official; genuine disclosed frequent-reporting-to-President requirement (Section 6).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Despite the 'without limitation' framing, the underlying purpose (rescue, transportation, maintenance, relief, havens of refuge for victims of enemy oppression) is narrowly and specifically humanitarian, not open-ended.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-Cabinet-Secretary structure and reporting requirement constrain unilateral action.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically extremely significant: the War Refugee Board is credited with playing a major role in the rescue of tens of thousands of Jews and other victims of Nazi persecution in the war's final years (including support for Raoul Wallenberg's rescue efforts in Hungary), created substantially in response to internal government pressure over the United States' prior inadequate response to the Holocaust — one of the most historically consequential humanitarian institutions of the entire war, a significant second/third-order effect even though its formal governance-architecture footprint (a small three-Secretary board) is otherwise modest.

title: Executive Order 9418—Authorizing the War Food Administration to Place Orders With Other Agencies for Materials or Services to Be Obtained by Contract or Otherwise

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, technical extension of existing inter-agency procurement-order authority (already vested in War/Navy/Treasury/CAA/Maritime Commission) to the War Food Administration, implementing existing 1920/1932/1942 statutory framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in the underlying statutory framework.

title: Executive Order 9419—Bronze Star Medal

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard military-decoration-eligibility template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed eligibility criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9420—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Arkwright Corporation, Berkshire Fine Spinning Associates, Inc., Border City Manufacturing Company, Bourne Mills, Howard Arthur Mills, Richard Borden Manufacturing Company, and Sagamore Manufacturing Company, in and About Fall River, Massachusetts

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Matches the standard multi-plant seizure template (seven named companies), with a genuine EXPANSION: the Secretary may additionally seize any related textile facility in the area 'used or operated by' the named companies 'and in which strikes have occurred or are threatened' — a self-determined, anticipatory-trigger expansion, though still bounded to facilities connected to the named entities.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-agency cooperation directive (War Manpower Commission, Selective Service System, Department of Justice) — a real, disclosed coordination structure.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To the extent deemed desirable by him' and the anticipatory 'strikes have occurred or are threatened' language are self-determined standards.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed 60-day outer-bound tied to productive-efficiency restoration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Genuine disclosed multi-agency cooperation directive and War Labor Disputes Act Section 5 compliance requirement constrain the seizure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as part of the escalating pattern of textile-industry labor-unrest seizure responses in this period.

title: Executive Order 9422—Ratifying the Use by the Army Air Forces of Certain Optical Glass

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure retroactive ratification of a discrete, already-completed 6,120.25-lb optical-glass transfer; no forward-looking discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Single, fully disclosed transaction; nothing to check going forward.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight channel is removed or narrowed.

title: Executive Order 9423—Transfer of the War Relocation Authority to the Department of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow machinery-transfer order: the already-existing War Relocation Authority and the Director's functions are moved from the Office for Emergency Management to the Secretary of the Interior, a Senate-confirmed Cabinet officer subject to ordinary presidential and congressional accountability. The order does not create, expand, or redefine the WRA's substantive internment-administration authority; it only relocates the existing institution's housing within the executive branch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Ordinary Cabinet-level accountability replaces intra-EOP housing; no new discretion is created by the relocation itself.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight mechanism is removed; the transfer is purely administrative.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Note for the record, out of care given the subject matter: the War Relocation Authority administered the internment of Japanese Americans, one of the gravest civil-liberties episodes of the era. That program's substantive authority derives from EO 9066 and EO 9102 (both outside this batch's date range and, per the corpus, from a different coding window), not from this order. EO 9423 itself is a housekeeping transfer of an already-existing agency between two executive locations and does not itself expand, contract, or redesign the internment apparatus. Consistent with the rubric's focus on structural design rather than programmatic morality, this order is scored on its own narrow textual terms; the historical gravity of the underlying WRA program is flagged here for the record rather than reflected in the numeric score, exactly as EO 9417 (War Refugee Board) was flagged in the prior batch for the inverse reason (high moral significance, modest structure).

title: Executive Order 9424—Establishing in the United States Patent Office a Register of Government Interests in Patents and Applications for Patents

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Secretary of Commerce directed to establish a records register; a narrow administrative/record-keeping function.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Ordinary Cabinet accountability; register open to inspection as a baseline rule.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single, narrow purpose (a patent-interest register); no bundling of distinct grants.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight channel is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Limited downstream effect: a records-transparency mechanism, not a policy lever.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Paragraph 3 makes the register's baseline openness contingent on a self-certifying carve-out: entries 'should be maintained in secrecy' at the sole discretion of 'the department or agency submitting them for recording,' with no named reviewing authority or standard cabining that judgment beyond the temporary restriction pending the National Patent Planning Commission's report. This is a disclosed but non-binding, agency-self-graded exemption from the register's own transparency purpose.

title: Executive Order 9426—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants, Facilities, Installations and Other Properties in California and Nevada of the Department of Water and Power of the City of Los Angeles, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of War is granted open-ended seizure/operation authority — 'to do all things necessary for or incidental to the operation thereof' — over an entire municipal utility's water AND power infrastructure spanning two states (California and Nevada), a broader single-official grant than the single-plant seizure template used elsewhere in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board or reporting requirement; the Secretary alone determines both the triggering interruption (Whereas clause) and the terminating restoration of service (Sec. 6) — a nominal, self-graded check rather than a genuine independent one.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles water production, water distribution, power generation, power distribution/transmission, AND communications systems — multiple distinct utility functions across two states — into a single seizure trigger, rather than a single plant/product line as in the Jenkins Brothers/Ken-Rad-style orders below.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary for the successful prosecution of the war, and to do all things necessary for or incidental to the operation thereof' is an open-ended standard with no enumerated boundary.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 6's termination trigger (restoration of efficiency, self-determined) functions like the other seizure orders in this batch but is captured under Flag 6 rather than scored as a distinct perverse-incentive structure here; no additional zombie-trap feature beyond the standard self-certified end-date pattern.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Possession terminates 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration of pre-interruption efficiency — a self-graded, non-fixed-date sunset rather than a binding external one.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy or creditor-remedy bar comparable to EO 9412/EO 9462 is present in this order's text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A two-state municipal utility seizure — water AND power for a major metropolitan area — carries broader downstream civilian-welfare exposure than a single manufacturing plant seizure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Paragraph 2 directs 'all federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate with the Secretary of War 'to the fullest extent possible' — an open-ended, unenumerated multi-agency subordination clause rather than a defined coordination channel.

title: Executive Order 9428—Authorizing Certain Former Employees of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, Department of Justice, to Acquire a Competitive Classified Status

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, retrospective civil-service-status remedy for a defined class of former FBI Fingerprint Classifiers hired 1939-1941; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective eligibility criteria (a)-(c); recommended by the Postmaster General/CSC-analogous process is absent here but eligibility is self-executing on defined facts.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9429—Amending Executive Order 9406 of December 17, 1943

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware, technical amendment reallocating Necessity Certificate processing between the Secretaries of War/Navy and the WPB Chairman strictly by application filing-date; it does not expand any official's discretionary authority beyond what EO 9406 already established (outside this batch's coding window).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Processing lines are objectively defined by filing date and facility-construction-commencement date, not official discretion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, the amendment narrows the War/Navy Secretaries' residual authority to already-pending applications.

title: Executive Order 9431—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine amendment of Foreign Service housekeeping regulations (place of residence, office hours) delegated to the Secretary of State under a pre-existing 1939 regulatory framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Ordinary Cabinet-level rulemaking, disclosed and published as regulation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9433—Extending the Limits of Certain Customs Ports of Entry

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure geographic boundary-extension for three customs ports; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, surveyed boundary metes-and-bounds.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9434—Naval Radio Station, Summit, Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Reservation of a defined 320.9-acre tract for naval use, superseding two prior specific orders; a bounded, surveyed land-use designation, not an open-ended power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 2(a) preserves a genuine disclosed check: the area 'shall continue to be subject to the civil jurisdiction of the Canal Zone Government,' i.e., naval control does not displace ordinary civil governance.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Civil jurisdiction is explicitly preserved rather than preempted.

title: Executive Order 9435—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Jenkins Brothers, Incorporated, at Bridgeport, Connecticut

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template: the Secretary of the Navy is granted open-ended possession/operation authority ('to do all things necessary for, or incidental to, the production, sale, and distribution of the products thereof') plus the power to 'exercise any contractual or other rights of the Corporation.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Secretary alone certifies both the triggering threat (Whereas clause) and, per Sec. 4, the restoration determination ending possession — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Narrow, single-company, single-site seizure; no bundling of distinct grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To take such other steps as he deems necessary to carry out the provisions and purposes of this Order' is an open, undefined standard.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No independent perverse-incentive structure beyond the standard self-certified termination captured under Flag 6.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Possession terminates 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration of productive efficiency — a self-graded, non-fixed-date sunset.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Secretary must 'observe the terms and conditions of the directive order of the National War Labor Board, dated February 9, 1944' as the substantive basis for the seizure, but the seizure itself provides the company no further channel to contest that already-issued WLB directive; possession becomes the enforcement mechanism for a labor ruling the company had declined to honor, foreclosing further review of the underlying wage dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single mid-sized manufacturer, single site; limited downstream/cascading exposure.

title: Executive Order 9436—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Ken-Rad Tube and Lamp Corporation and Ken-Rad Transmitting Tube Corporation at Owensboro, Kentucky

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Same standard single-seizure template as EO 9435, here covering two affiliated corporations at one site, with open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies both trigger and termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Two affiliated corporations at a single site under one directive order is not meaningfully distinct bundling beyond the standard single-seizure template.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do all the things necessary for, or incidental to, the production, sale, and distribution of the products thereof' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: possession terminates 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive order of the National War Labor Board, dated March 31, 1944' with no further review channel for the underlying wage dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Two affiliated tube manufacturers, single locality; modest downstream exposure.

title: Executive Order 9438—Authorizing the Secretary of Commerce to Take Possession of and to Operate the Plants and Facilities of Montgomery Ward and Company, Located in Chicago, Illinois

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A structural anomaly among this batch's seizure orders: authority is vested in the SECRETARY OF COMMERCE — a civilian department with no organic enforcement or security apparatus — rather than War or Navy as in every other seizure order this batch, covering the mail-order house, retail store, AND Schwinn Warehouse together.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Commerce self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination — nominal check only, and notably weaker in practice given Commerce's lack of an organic enforcement arm to actually secure compliance (addressed by paragraph 4's cross-agency assistance clause below).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles three distinct facility types (mail-order house, retail store, warehouse) into a single seizure trigger.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do all things necessary or incidental thereto' and 'any other steps that he deems necessary' are open, undefined standards.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure is explicitly grounded in the company's 'failure... to comply with directive orders of the National War Labor Board dated January 15, 1944, and April 5, 1944' — the seizure operates as the enforcement bludgeon for two already-issued labor rulings the company had refused to honor (this is the order underlying the famous Sewell Avery incident, in which the Montgomery Ward chairman was physically carried from his office by soldiers), with no further channel to contest the underlying directives.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A nationally prominent mail-order/retail chain; the seizure attracted major contemporaneous public and legal controversy over executive seizure power's reach into a company whose noncompliance was a labor-relations dispute rather than a war-production shortfall.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Paragraph 4: 'Upon the request of the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of War shall take any action that may be necessary to enable the Secretary of Commerce to carry out the provisions and purposes of this order' — an explicit, open-ended borrowing of the War Department's enforcement capacity by a civilian department, the clause that in practice authorized the Army's physical involvement in the seizure.

title: Executive Order 9439—Establishing a Uniform Monthly Rate of Pay for Student Nurses Transferred to Federal Hospitals

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Sets a single, fixed, statutorily-bounded stipend rate; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed rate and termination condition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9440—Authorizing the Reconstruction Finance Corporation to Place Orders With Other Agencies for Materials or Services to Be Obtained by Contract or Otherwise

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an already-existing statutory procurement mechanism (previously vested in War, Navy, Treasury, CAA, Maritime Commission, and the War Food Administration) to an additional named agency (RFC and its designated subsidiaries); does not create new discretionary authority, only adds a new holder of an existing, narrowly-defined tool.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The underlying procurement mechanism and its statutory basis are fully disclosed and already established by prior law/orders.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Consolidates references to four distinct statutory provisions (1920 Act as amended 1932, 1942 Act, and EO 9418) into a single grant of procurement authority to RFC, and the closing sentence broadly supersedes 'any provision of any executive order or proclamation conflicting with this order... to the extent of such conflict' — a general, unenumerated supersession clause.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight of the underlying procurement mechanism is removed; RFC simply gains access to a tool other agencies already use.

title: Executive Order 9441—Suspension of Eight-Hour Law as to Laborers and Mechanics Employed by the Veterans' Administration on Public Work Within the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow statutory suspension (extending overtime hours) applicable to a defined class of VA laborers/mechanics; the 'Extraordinary Emergency' finding is a standard, judicially-recognized predicate under the 1892 Act rather than a novel concentration of power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed check is preserved: the Proviso requires wages and overtime pay to 'be computed in accordance with the provisions of existing law' — the underlying wage-protection statute is not suspended, only the hours cap.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The suspension runs 'for the duration of the emergencies proclaimed by me on September 8, 1939, and May 27, 1941' — by May 1944 these proclamations were already four-and-a-half and three years old respectively, with no independent review date; the labor protection remains suspended for as long as the emergency proclamations themselves remain formally un-terminated, the classic zombie-emergency pattern.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed expiration date; termination is entirely contingent on formal revocation of the 1939/1941 emergency proclamations, which is itself undefined and open-ended.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No further oversight beyond the preserved wage-computation requirement is removed.

title: Executive Order 9442—Waiving the Provisions of Executive Order of January 17, 1873, as to Eugene E. Dickerson, Postmaster at Warmsprings, Montana

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized dual-office-holding waiver for one named postmaster, extended to future incumbents of that specific post; no general discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed rationale (mutual state/federal advantage) and recommended by the Postmaster General with CSC concurrence — a genuine, disclosed multi-party sign-off.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9443—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and to Operate the Plants and Facilities of Hummer Manufacturing Division of Montgomery Ward & Company, Inc., Located at Springfield, Illinois

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-seizure template, here notably a SECOND Montgomery Ward seizure (a division in Springfield, Illinois) following EO 9438's Chicago seizure by four weeks — same open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority, now vested in the Secretary of War rather than Commerce.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 4, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single division, single site; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing[] that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure is grounded in 'failure of Montgomery Ward & Company, Inc., to comply with a directive order of the National War Labor Board dated April 14, 1944' — again the seizure enforces an already-issued labor ruling with no further review channel.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Part of a recurring pattern of federal seizures targeting the same corporate parent (Montgomery Ward) within a single month, illustrating how the plant-seizure mechanism could be deployed repeatedly against a single defiant employer across multiple of its facilities.

title: Executive Order 9444—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, Declared Value Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns by the Special Committee on Un-American Activities, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants a congressional committee inspection ACCESS to otherwise-confidential tax returns for a defined statutory investigative purpose (House Resolution 65); this enhances, rather than concentrates, oversight capacity, and is bounded by Treasury-prescribed rules and regulations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: inspection is conditioned on compliance with 'rules and regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury in Treasury Decision 5200, as amended.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9445—Designating the Honorable Martin Travieso, Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Puerto Rico, as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico to Act in the Case of United States v. Julio Trinidad Geigel

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized, single-case judicial-designation order, triggered by the sitting judge's disqualification; no discretionary power grant beyond the narrow statutory designation authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed rationale (recusal) and scope (one named case).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, this ensures a case is not left unheard.

title: Executive Order 9446—Regulations Governing the Allowance of Travel Expenses of Claimants and Beneficiaries of the Veterans' Administration and of Their Attendants

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes fixed, statutorily-bounded travel-reimbursement rules for VA claimants/beneficiaries; the 'may authorize' discretion is a routine benefits-administration function, not a power concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective reimbursement structure (actual expenses or 3-cents-per-mile allowance).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Explicitly supersedes and replaces the narrower EO 8454 rather than removing any oversight.

title: Executive Order 9447—Amendment of Executive Order 9356 of June 24, 1943, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow rate-table amendment to an already-existing clothing-allowance schedule; purely arithmetic/administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9448—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns by the Select Committee to Investigate the Federal Communications Commission, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants a congressional investigative committee inspection access to historical (1932-1938) tax returns for a defined statutory purpose (House Resolution 21); oversight-enhancing rather than power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: inspection is conditioned on compliance with Treasury-prescribed rules and regulations approved contemporaneously.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9449—Amending Regulations Concerning Foreign Service Pay Adjustment

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment to a currency-fluctuation loss-payment regulation, excluding certain military-attached personnel who are governed by an alternative paragraph; routine administrative line-drawing.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9450—Amending Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes/amends the composition of two Foreign Service internal boards (Board of Examiners, Board of Foreign Service Officers' Training School); a multi-department representative body, not a single-official concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-party check: the Board of Examiners is deliberately composed across State, Commerce, Agriculture, and the Civil Service Commission's Chief Examiner, and the Training School Board's decisions are 'subject to the approval of the Secretary of State' — real cross-departmental representation rather than a single official's fiat.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the multi-department board structure is itself a check.

title: Executive Order 9451—Revoking Paragraph 2 of Executive Order 9243 of September 12, 1942, Entitled "Providing for the Transfer and Release of Federal Personnel"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A one-paragraph revocation of a prior priority-classification provision; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order removes a prior mechanism rather than removing oversight of one.

title: Executive Order 9452—Authorizing the Secretary of State to Prescribe Regulations and Issue Orders and Instructions Relating to the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A broad, standing delegation: the Secretary of State may 'prescribe such regulations and issue such orders and instructions... as he may deem conducive to the public interest' governing the duties and business conduct of the entire Foreign Service — an open-ended, self-defined standard rather than an enumerated list of specific regulatory subjects.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed check survives: paragraph 2 expressly preserves 'the legal requirements relating to publication in the Federal Register,' and the grant is expressly bounded — it 'shall not be exercised in any case in which the President is specifically authorized by law to prescribe regulations with respect to a particular subject,' a real textual limitation on the delegation's scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem conducive to the public interest' is a standard entirely defined by the Secretary's own judgment, with no enumerated criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Federal Register publication and the particular-subject carve-out are genuine, disclosed limits, not removed.

title: Executive Order 9453—Participation by the United States in the Work of the United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The FEA Administrator is authorized to exercise, 'through any executive department, independent establishment, or agency,' all functions and authority over UNRRA-related fund expenditure and supply provision vested in the President by two separate 1944 statutes — a broad delegable fund-expenditure authority routed through a single Administrator.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Multiple genuine, disclosed checks are retained: exercise is expressly 'subject to such policies and directions as the President may from time to time prescribe,' subject to the terms of the already-concluded November 9, 1943 UNRRA Agreement, and activities 'shall be carried on in conformity with the foreign policy of the United States as defined by the Secretary of State' — three independent, textually-disclosed constraints rather than a nominal one.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles fund-expenditure authority, supply/service provision, and U.S. representation on the UNRRA Council and its subcommittees into a single Administrator's remit.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The retained presidential-direction, treaty-conformity, and State-Department foreign-policy-conformity requirements are genuine, disclosed limits on the delegation, not oversight removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: UNRRA became the principal Allied vehicle for liberated-territory relief and rehabilitation in 1944-46, a historically significant multilateral commitment channeled through a single wartime domestic agency (FEA).

title: Executive Order 9454—Temporary Appointments as Officers in the Army of the United States of Members of the Army Nurse Corps and Female Dietetic and Physical-Therapy Personnel of the Medical Department of the Army

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A blanket but narrowly-defined personnel-status conversion (Army Nurse Corps and dietetic/physical-therapy personnel to commissioned rank) under a specific 1944 statute; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, self-executing eligibility on defined facts (active duty on June 22, 1944).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9455—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, Declared Value Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns by the Select Committee to Investigate the Federal Communications Commission, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends the EO 9448 tax-return-inspection grant to later tax years (1939-1944); oversight-enhancing, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: subject to Treasury decision conditions approved contemporaneously; explicitly 'supplemental to Executive Order 9448.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9456—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow statutory-provision suspension tied to three specific 1945 appropriation acts governing Canal Zone employment; not a general power concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Disclosed statutory basis; suspension scope is defined by the underlying appropriation acts' provisions rather than open executive discretion.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Suspension runs 'during the continuance of any of the wars in which the United States is now engaged' — an open-ended wartime duration with no fixed date and no independent review trigger, the same zombie-emergency structure as EO 9441.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed expiration; termination is contingent on the informal, undefined end of 'any of the wars... now engaged,' not a date-certain or externally-reviewed trigger.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No additional oversight beyond the underlying appropriation acts' own terms is removed.

title: Executive Order 9457—Reinstatement of Philip W. Bonsai in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Official text was not published (per the corpus and the Federal Register record); based on the disclosed title, this is an individualized personnel-reinstatement action of the same narrow, non-discretionary type as the routine Foreign Service personnel orders coded elsewhere in this batch, and is coded conservatively on that basis.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Individualized personnel action; nothing in the disclosed title suggests any discretionary or structural grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is implicated by a single-person reinstatement.

title: Executive Order 9458—Amending Paragraphs 6 and 7 of Executive Order No. 9195 of July 7, 1942, Prescribing Regulations Relating to Aerial Flights by Personnel of the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, and National Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow procedural amendment to flight-duty order/revocation reporting chains across the armed services; administrative housekeeping.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed reporting chain to named service officials.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9460—Regulations Relating to Glider Flights by Personnel of the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, and Coast Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific 1944 pay-differential statute with objective flight-count/duration requirements and defined exception procedures (equipment unavailability, combat conditions, injury); a narrow, largely self-executing benefits-administration order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective criteria; exceptions require a commanding officer's certification, a genuine, if routine, disclosed check.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9461-A—Designating the Honorable A. Cecil Snyder as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States For Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized, standing (calendar-year) judicial-designation order for general absence/illness/disability coverage, expressly carved out from — and not affecting — the single-case EO 9445 designation; narrow statutory designation authority only.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope, explicitly reconciled against the overlapping EO 9445 designation to avoid ambiguity.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9462—Possession and Operation of Certain Motor Carrier Transportation Systems

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted sweeping seizure/operation authority — 'to do anything that he may deem necessary to carry out the provisions and purposes of this order' — over the transportation systems of 103 separately-named motor carriers spanning at least a dozen Midwestern and Plains states, the broadest single-official grant of any order in this chunk.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat, and, per Sec. 4, alone determines termination when possession/control/operation are 'no longer necessary' — a nominal, self-graded check over an industry-wide seizure rather than a genuinely independent one.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles 103 separately-named, independently-owned motor-carrier businesses across at least twelve states into a single seizure trigger with no differentiated review per carrier — the most extensive multi-entity bundling by carrier count of any single-labor-dispute-triggered seizure order in this batch (compare EO 9463's five companies and EO 9466's ~124 companies, both narrower geographically).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do anything that he may deem necessary' is a wholly open-ended standard with no enumerated boundary.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 4's termination trigger ('when he determines that such possession, control, and operation are no longer necessary for the successful prosecution of the war') is entirely self-judged, with no fixed date and no external review — the seizure can persist indefinitely at the Director's sole discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 explicitly bars, 'except with the prior written consent of the Director,' any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against any property in the Director's possession — a direct, textual creditor/judicial-remedy preemption clause, structurally identical in kind to EO 9412's Section 8 railroad-seizure bar from the prior batch, here extended to an entire industry segment rather than a single nationalized system.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 2 provides that WLB-directive wage increases which accrued prior to the taking of possession may be paid by the Director 'only out of the net operating revenue of the said system' — tying affected workers' back-pay recovery to the financial performance of a system now under government seizure, a disclosed but real third-party financial-risk-shifting structure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A 103-carrier, multi-state seizure of general freight motor-transport capacity carries substantial cascading supply-chain exposure across the wartime Midwest, well beyond a single-plant seizure's downstream reach.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: 'Upon the request of the Director... the Secretary of War is authorized to take any action that may be necessary to enable the Director to carry out the provisions and purposes of this order' — an open-ended cross-agency assistance clause paralleling EO 9438's Commerce/War arrangement, now applied to an entire transportation sector.

title: Executive Order 9463—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Pacific Gear and Tool Works, the Federal Mogul Corporation, the Link-Belt Company (Pacific Division), the U.S. Pipe and Manufacturing Company, and the Enterprise Engine and Foundry Company, Located at San Francisco, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard seizure template extended across FIVE separately-named companies at San Francisco under a single order, with open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of the Navy.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination across all five companies — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles five distinct, independently-owned manufacturing companies into a single seizure trigger and single directive-order compliance obligation, rather than the single-company template used in EO 9435/9436/9443.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the Directive Order of the Tenth Regional War Labor Board dated April 20, 1944, as modified by the Directive Order of the National War Labor Board dated June 3, 1944' across all five companies, with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Five San Francisco-area manufacturers seized in a single coordinated action, reflecting the Bay Area's concentrated 1944 labor-disturbance/seizure activity (paralleled the same week by EO 9466's much larger San Francisco machine-shop-sector seizure below).

title: Executive Order 9464—Transferring the Use, Possession, and Control of Certain Land in the Nantahala National Forest from the Department of Agriculture to the Tennessee Valley Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, surveyed 4.4-acre land-jurisdiction transfer between two federal bodies under pre-existing statutory authority (1933 TVA Act, 1897 Act); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed metes-and-bounds description; transfer 'subject to such rights as may be vested' in the county/state for road rights-of-way — a genuine, disclosed preserved limitation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Local road rights-of-way are explicitly preserved rather than preempted.

title: Executive Order 9465—Amendment of Executive Order 9356 of June 24, 1943, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-subsection rate-table amendment to an already-existing clothing-allowance schedule; purely arithmetic/administrative, the third such amendment to EO 9356 in this batch (following EO 9447).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9466—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Certain Machine Shop Companies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard seizure template extended across a very large, attached list of San Francisco-area machine-shop and light-manufacturing companies — two enumerated lists totaling approximately 124 separately-named firms — with open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of the Navy over the entire named group at once.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Secretary alone certifies the triggering interruption and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination for the entire 124-firm group — nominal, self-graded check only, applied uniformly across firms of widely varying size with no individualized findings disclosed in the order text beyond the general Whereas recital.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles roughly 124 separately-owned, independently-operated companies — ranging from major firms like American Can Co. and Fairbanks Morse to single-proprietor machine shops — into one seizure trigger under a single Whereas finding and a single directive-order compliance obligation, the largest entity-count bundle encountered in this batch and among the largest in the stream to date; no company-specific findings are disclosed.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended, applied uniformly across the entire 124-firm list.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date, and notably must be separately assessed by the Secretary across ~124 firms of disparate size and recovery timelines with no disclosed individualized procedure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the Directive Order of the Tenth Regional War Labor Board dated April 20, 1944, as modified by the Directive Order of the National War Labor Board dated June 3, 1944' — the same regional wage dispute underlying EO 9463 one week earlier, now extended to the entire San Francisco machine-shop sector, with no further review channel.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: An entire regional industrial sector (San Francisco machine-shop/light-manufacturing) placed under seizure in a single order is a qualitatively larger structural event than any single- or few-company seizure in this batch, with correspondingly larger downstream effects on the region's wartime manufacturing supply base and on the ~124 affected firms' ordinary commercial operations and creditor/customer relationships.

title: Executive Order 9468—Transfer of Certain Personnel Among the Coast and Geodetic Survey and the War and Navy Departments

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A reciprocal, narrowly-defined personnel-transfer mechanism between the Coast and Geodetic Survey and the War/Navy Departments, contingent on mutual agreement between the Secretary of Commerce and the Secretary of War or Navy; not a unilateral power concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: transfers require 'mutual agreement between the Secretary of Commerce and the Secretary of War or the Secretary of the Navy,' a real cross-departmental consent requirement, and Sec. 4 requires return to the Survey 'when the present national emergency ceases to exist' as the default rule.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Mutual-consent and default-return provisions are genuine, disclosed limits, not oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9469—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and Operate the Mines, Collieries, and Facilities of the Philadelphia and Reading Coal and Iron Company in the State of Pennsylvania

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template: Secretary of the Interior granted open-ended possession/operation authority ('to do all things necessary for, or incidental to, the production, sale and distribution of the coal') over the named company's mines, collieries, and preparation facilities.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 4, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named company; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy bar or specific WLB-directive-enforcement clause is present in this order's text; Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally rather than a specific already-issued directive.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 3 directs 'all Federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, the War Department, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate — an open-ended, unenumerated multi-agency subordination clause.

title: Executive Order 9470—Establishing a Method of Determining the Amounts of Bonds of Collectors of Customs

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A uniform, formula-based bonding methodology (10% of average monthly collections, $10,000-$100,000 range) for customs collectors; narrow administrative standardization.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, numeric formula, with periodic (biennial) mandatory review.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything the mandatory biennial examination is itself a disclosed check.

title: Executive Order 9471—Disposition of the Functions and Termination of the Division of Central Administrative Services of the Office for Emergency Management

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Bureau of the Budget is granted broad, standing discretion to 'make an administrative determination with respect to the disposition to be made of each function' of a terminated OEM division, including ordering discontinuance of functions outright, and 'such other administrative determinations as may be required' — an open-ended personal-discretion clause governing an entire agency's wind-down.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Grounded in and bounded by the National War Agency Appropriation Act, 1945's own provisions for the Division; the Director's discretion operates within a congressionally-set wind-down framework, and CSC retains its own determination authority over personnel disposition — a genuine, disclosed statutory boundary.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may determine' and 'such other administrative determinations as may be required' are open, undefined standards governing the disposition of an entire agency's functions, personnel, and property.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: CSC's personnel-disposition authority is expressly preserved rather than displaced.

title: Executive Order 9472—Establishing Certain Awards for the Merchant Marine

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes honorary citations, a meritorious service medal, and service ribbons for Merchant Marine personnel; a symbolic recognition program, not a coercive power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Award criteria (outstanding/gallant action, meritorious conduct) are administered 'under such regulations as the Administrator may prescribe' — routine program administration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9473—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the International Nickel Company, Inc., Located in and Around Huntington, West Virginia

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 6, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 6: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited as the enforcement basis, unlike several peer orders in this batch.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single major nickel producer; modest, largely localized downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4 directs 'all federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, the Department of Justice, and the Office of Price Administration' to cooperate — an open-ended, unenumerated multi-agency subordination clause, here notably including OPA.

title: Executive Order 9475-A—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate Certain Plants and Facilities of the Hughes Tool Company Located at Houston, Texas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: CORPUS/DB NUMBERING MISMATCH DISCLOSED: the DB record for EO 9475 carries the title 'Executive Order 9475-A—...Hughes Tool Company Located at Houston, Texas' (Sept. 2, 1944), but the locally-scraped corpus file at EO-9475.json contains an unrelated order, 'Executive Order 9475—Transferring the Functions and Responsibilities of the Rubber Director' (Sept. 1, 1944) — the same '-A'/'-B' suffix/base-number divergence pattern previously identified for EO 9242/9242-A and EO 9395/9395-B. Consistent with that precedent, this record is coded via title-pattern-matching against the disclosed DB title, applying the standard single-company plant-seizure template established across this batch (Secretary of War given open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority over the named company's plants and facilities).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Standard nominal self-certified check, consistent with the seizure template (no external board disclosed in the title alone, and none is typical for this order class).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named company; standard single-seizure scope, per the template.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Standard open-ended 'as he deems necessary' operational discretion typical of this order class.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination, per the template.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standard self-graded, non-fixed-date termination ('within sixty days after he determines' restoration), per the template.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy bar or other oversight-preemption language is evidenced by the disclosed title; coded conservatively as ABSENT absent full text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure, consistent with peer single-company seizures this batch.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: No disclosed cross-agency assistance clause is evidenced by the title alone; coded conservatively as ABSENT absent full text.

title: Executive Order 9478—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and Operate the Mines, Collieries, and Preparation Facilities of Certain Coal Companies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard seizure template extended across nine separately-named coal companies across Pennsylvania and West Virginia under a single order, open-ended possession/operation authority vested in the Secretary of the Interior over the entire named group.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination across all nine companies — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles nine distinct, independently-owned coal companies across two states into a single seizure trigger under one general Whereas finding.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement

flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.
