flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date, separately assessable across sixteen sites with no disclosed individualized procedure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The largest single coal-seizure order in this batch by mine count; part of the sustained wave of coal-sector labor unrest across September 1944.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 3 directs 'all Federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, the War Department, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate — open-ended, unenumerated.

title: Executive Order 9483—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and Operate Certain Mines, Collieries, and Preparation Facilities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard seizure template extended across seven separately-named mines across West Virginia under a single order, open-ended possession/operation authority vested in the Secretary of the Interior.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination across all seven mines — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles seven distinct mine operations (three under common ownership, Consolidation Coal Co.) into a single seizure trigger under one general Whereas finding.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he deems necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' is open-ended, applied across the full seven-mine list.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The fourth coal-seizure order within a single month (following EO 9469, 9478, 9481, 9482), underscoring the depth and persistence of the September 1944 coal-labor crisis.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 3 directs 'all Federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, the War Department, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate — open-ended, unenumerated.

title: Executive Order 9485—Relinquishing Possession of the Plant and Facilities of Remington Rand Inc. in the Town of Southport, County of Chemung, State of New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A termination/relinquishment order ending the government's possession under the already-scored EO 9399 (outside this batch's coding window); narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: relinquishment is grounded in the President's own determination, following a private contractual arrangement between the United States and the company, that the plant 'will be operated in a manner consistent with the war effort' — an affirmative, disclosed restoration-of-private-control finding.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes oversight/private control.

title: Executive Order 9487—Regulations Governing the Issuance of Non-Necessity Certificates Under Section 124(d) of the Internal Revenue Code

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the WPB Chairman (as 'certifying authority') with the operative determination of when a wartime facility 'ceased... to be necessary in the interest of national defense' — a determination with direct tax consequences for the facility's owner, made on the certifying authority's own judgment of 'no presently foreseeable substantial need.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed process constraint exists: certification issues 'promptly... upon proper application,' the standard is tied to objective facility condition/location/ownership factors, and Sec. 3 allows the certifying authority to obtain outside agency reports/recommendations as part of the determination — a real, if modest, disclosed procedural structure rather than unbounded fiat.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'No presently foreseeable substantial need... exists' is a judgment-laden standard without further definition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed; this order implements, rather than displaces, a statutory tax-certification scheme.

title: Executive Order 9486—Transfer of Functions With Respect to Non-Necessity Certificates From the Secretary of War and the Secretary of the Navy to the Chairman of the War Production Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, diff-aware jurisdictional transfer of a single statutory certification function (IRC §124(d)) from the War/Navy Secretaries to the WPB Chairman; does not expand the function's substantive scope, only its holder, paralleling EO 9490 below and mirroring the earlier EO 9429/9406 pattern from this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis and scope; the companion regulatory order (EO 9487) discloses the operative standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, centralizing this function in the WPB Chairman (who already held the parallel §124(f) necessity-certificate function per this batch's EO 9429/9406 chain) reduces jurisdictional fragmentation.

title: Executive Order 9488—Transfer of the Records, Property, Funds and Personnel of the Office of War Mobilization and Its Constituent Agencies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A statutorily-compelled machinery transfer implementing the War Mobilization and Reconversion Act of 1944: OWM (established by the already-scored EO 9347, outside this window), the Surplus War Property Administration, and the Retraining and Reemployment Administration are transferred into their statutory successor bodies (OWMR, Surplus Property Board, Retraining and Reemployment Administration) as those bodies come into being. Congress, not the President, defines the successor structure; this order implements the statute's own transition mechanics.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed statutory anchor: each transfer's timing and scope is tied to 'section 605 of the War Mobilization and Reconversion Act of 1944' and to the Bureau of the Budget Director's administrative determinations of available funds — a real, disclosed procedural structure rather than unbounded executive fiat.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the order carries out Congress's own reorganization statute.

title: Executive Order 9489—Authorizing and Directing the Secretary of War to Designate a Military Commander for the Territory of Hawaii and Authorizing the Military Commander to Prescribe Said Territory, or Any Part Thereof, as a Military Area, and for Other Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A single military commander (designated by the Secretary of War) is authorized to prescribe all or any part of the Territory of Hawaii as a 'military area' and, within it, to control 'the right of any person to enter, remain in, or leave' the area — a sweeping, self-triggered civil-liberties-affecting power vested in one designated officer over an entire U.S. territory. For context: this order was issued as Hawaii's broader December 1941 martial-law regime was being formally wound down (ended later in October 1944); it establishes a narrower successor military-area/exclusion framework rather than initiating military rule from a standing start, which somewhat qualifies but does not eliminate the concentration this text vests.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Each specific power (curfew, evacuation, exclusion, detention incident thereto, press/radio regulation, travel restriction) is triggered solely 'whenever military necessity therefor exists and the military commander so finds' — the commander's own determination is the only disclosed check on the exercise of exclusion and detention authority; the Secretary of War's role is limited to designating who holds the office, not reviewing how the power is used. Consistent with this rubric's convention, a fully self-graded, unchecked authority over detention/exclusion of persons is scored CRITICAL rather than merely nominal.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles an extraordinarily wide range of distinct police-power domains into a single designation: blackout/curfew, air-raid precautions, enemy-alien conduct regulation, evacuation/exclusion/detention, firearms regulation, foreign military-service registration, port/harbor/cargo security, travel restriction, and foreign-language press/radio regulation — a CRITICAL-level bundle exceeding any other order in this batch.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Whenever military necessity therefor exists and the military commander so finds' is repeated as the self-determined trigger for nearly every enumerated power, with no further definition of 'military necessity.'

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5 provides a genuine, if not date-certain, eventual termination: the authority 'shall expire thirty days after the cessation of hostilities between the empire of Japan and the United States' — an objectively determinable future event (formal cessation of hostilities) rather than an indefinitely-renewable presidential proclamation, distinguishing this order favorably on this narrow point from the pure zombie-emergency pattern (e.g., EO 9441, EO 9456) elsewhere in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 4 explicitly ties this order's military-area/exclusion framework to Executive Order No. 9066 of February 19, 1942 (outside this batch's coding window, the order underlying the mainland Japanese American exclusion program): any military-area designation under this order 'shall constitute designation... for the purposes of the said Executive Order No. 9066... and shall vest the military commander with the powers provided in that order.' No judicial-review channel for exclusion/detention determinations is disclosed anywhere in the text. Given the gravity and historical significance of this cross-reference, it is flagged here for the record with appropriate care: EO 9066-derived exclusion/detention authority carries the same disclosed absence of a judicial check when extended to Hawaii as it did on the mainland, even though its practical application in the Hawaiian theater — where roughly one-third of the territory's population was of Japanese ancestry, an economically and logistically decisive difference from the mainland — differed substantially from the mainland internment program in actual scale and implementation.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends EO 9066-type exclusion/detention legal authority to an entire U.S. territory at a point (October 1944) when the war's outcome was no longer in serious doubt, raising significant historical and civil-liberties second-order concerns distinct from, but related to, the mainland internment program; scored CRITICAL for the combination of civil-liberties stakes and the order's cross-referencing incorporation of EO 9066's own unreviewed exclusion framework.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 3 delegates to the military commander the President's own Title III Second War Powers Act authority over Hawaii-specific port/harbor/transportation priorities and allocation — a delegation of presidential economic-command authority to a military, rather than civilian, official.

title: Executive Order 9490—Transfer of Functions of the Secretary of War and the Secretary of the Navy Under Section 124 of the Internal Revenue Code, to the Chairman of the War Production Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Consolidates all remaining War/Navy Secretary functions under IRC §124 (necessity-certificate amendment, §124(h) payment-certificate determinations) into the WPB Chairman, who already held the parallel functions transferred by EO 9486/9429; a narrow jurisdictional consolidation, not a new grant of substantive power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory scope ('not heretofore transferred... under section 124').

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; centralizes an already-fragmented function.

title: Executive Order 9491—Regulations Governing the Issuance of Payment Certificates Under Section 124(H) of the Internal Revenue Code

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the WPB Chairman (as 'certifying authority') with the operative determination of whether a compensation payment to a taxpayer qualifies for a payment certificate under two enumerated statutory conditions (contract termination, or reasonable-but-unfulfilled expectation of future contracts).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed procedural constraint: the certifying authority must obtain 'a certified statement from the officer of the contracting agency who authorized the payment' as proof of the statutory elements — a real, disclosed documentary check rather than unbounded fiat.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed; implements a statutory tax-certification scheme with a disclosed documentary-proof requirement.

title: Executive Order 9492—Regulations Governing Non-Military and Non-Naval Transportation on Army and Navy Air Transports

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The War and Navy Secretaries (and, for certain certifications, the State Department) are authorized to permit civilian passengers/cargo on military air transports, with military need retaining priority — a discretionary allocation of scarce wartime transport capacity to non-military uses.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Multiple genuine, disclosed checks: military/naval needs 'at all times have priority,' a market-rate charge floor is required (tied to CAB-filed commercial tariffs), and non-war-effort traffic is barred if 'reasonably be handled by a United States civil air carrier' — real, disclosed substantive limits on the discretion, not merely nominal ones.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 2(c) provides a genuine, fixed-date sunset: non-war-effort chargeable traffic (excluding lend-lease) 'shall be terminated one year from the date of this order,' with an EARLIER termination possible on any given route once the Civil Aeronautics Board certifies adequate commercial capacity — an unusually strong, externally-verifiable sunset mechanism, one of the cleanest disclosed sunset structures encountered in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The CAB certification role and the civil-carrier-priority requirement are genuine, disclosed checks preserved, not removed.

title: Executive Order 9493—Authorizing and Directing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of the Plants and Facilities of the Lord Manufacturing Company, Located at Erie, Pennsylvania

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A structurally distinct seizure trigger from the labor-dispute template used elsewhere in this batch: the Whereas recitals describe a REGULATORY-defiance basis — the company 'wilfully refused and failed to furnish the said war materials at the prices fixed' by a September 23, 1944 War/Navy Department price order issued under Section 801 of the Revenue Act of 1943, and had 'changed the terms and conditions of sale... to increase the return to the said company above the prices fixed.' The Secretary of the Navy is granted the same open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority as the labor-dispute seizures, now deployed to enforce price-control compliance.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Secretary alone certifies both the underlying price-defiance finding and, per Sec. 3, the eventual restoration determination — nominal, self-graded check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named company; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do all things necessary for or incidental to the production, sale, or distribution of the products of the said company' and 'such other steps as may be necessary or desirable' are open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 3's termination trigger — possession ends 'upon his determination that the said plant... will thereafter be operated in a manner consistent with the effective prosecution of the war' — carries NO 60-day outer cap after the restoration determination, unlike every labor-dispute seizure order elsewhere in this batch; termination is purely and indefinitely at the Secretary's own discretion with no disclosed backstop date at all.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed date and, distinctively among this batch's seizure orders, no 60-day outer bound on the post-restoration termination window either — the weakest disclosed sunset structure of any seizure order in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The seizure is explicitly deployed to override the company's 'wilfully' defiant response to an already-issued price-fixing order, foreclosing further price-dispute resolution through the ordinary Revenue Act Title VIII price-order enforcement channel in favor of outright possession — a preemption of the normal regulatory dispute path, paralleling in kind (though via a different statutory route) the WLB-directive-enforcement seizures elsewhere in this batch.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A distinctive trigger-type precedent: the first seizure in this batch predicated on regulatory (price-control) defiance rather than a labor dispute, targeting a supplier of 'aircraft parts and other essential war materials' whose noncompliance was found to directly threaten military procurement — worth flagging as a structurally notable variant on the seizure-power family.

title: Executive Order 9494—Authorizing the Civil Service Commission to Confer a Classified Status Upon Certain Employees of the Department of Justice

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized civil-service-status grant for three named Antitrust Division employees; narrow, non-discretionary personnel action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, named individuals; recommended jointly by the Attorney General and the Civil Service Commission — a genuine, disclosed multi-party sign-off.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9495—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9177 of May 30, 1942, to the United States Maritime Commission and the Administrator of the War Shipping Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an already-existing customs/duty-related delegation (EO 9177, outside this batch's coding window) to two additional named agencies, 'subject to the limitations contained in that order' — a bounded extension of an existing, previously-scored mechanism rather than a new grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Expressly bounded: 'subject to the limitations contained in that order' preserves whatever checks EO 9177 itself already discloses.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: EO 9177's own limitations are expressly preserved, not removed.

title: Executive Order 9496—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Certain Companies Located in or Near Toledo, Ohio

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard seizure template extended across seven separately-named Toledo-area companies (including Willys-Overland Motors) under a single order, open-ended possession/operation authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination across all seven companies — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles seven distinct, independently-owned companies into a single seizure trigger, notably covering both EXISTING and merely THREATENED interruptions across the group in a single finding.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' is open-ended, applied across the seven-company list.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Includes Willys-Overland Motors, the wartime Jeep manufacturer — a nationally significant defense contractor swept into a regional multi-company seizure order.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4 directs 'all Federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate — open-ended, unenumerated.

title: Executive Order 9497—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9039 Authorizing Sick and Rest Leave for Alien Employees of the Panama Canal and the Panama Railroad Company, and Correction of Section 4 of Executive Order No. 9467 Relating to Conditions of Employment in the Service of the Panama Canal and the Panama Railroad Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow leave-policy amendment (fixed 24-day annual / 60-day cumulative cap) and a technical cross-reference correction to a prior order; routine administrative housekeeping.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed numeric caps.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9499—Inspection of Income, Excess-Profits, Declared Value Excess-Profits, and Capital Stock Tax Returns by the Department of Commerce

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the Department of Commerce inspection access to tax returns (1941 and subsequent years) for statistical/economic-analysis purposes; oversight/analysis-enhancing, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: inspection is 'subject to the conditions in the Treasury decision... approved by me this date.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9498—Amending Executive Order 9079 of February 26, 1942, Making Certain Public Health Service Hospitals Available for the Care and Treatment of Insane Persons

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow interagency reimbursement-mechanics amendment; the Federal Security Administrator's 'to the extent deemed necessary and proper' discretion governs only the timing/method of an inter-appropriation payment, not substantive program authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed reimbursement mechanism and appropriations sourcing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9500—Extension of Trust Periods on Indian Lands Expiring the During Calendar Year 1945

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, beneficiary-favoring blanket extension (25 years) of trust status for Indian lands otherwise due to lose federal trust protection in 1945; extends an existing protective status rather than creating new discretionary power, and expressly carves out cases where 'Congress has specifically reserved to itself authority.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed 25-year extension term; the congressional-reservation carve-out is a genuine, disclosed limitation on the order's own reach.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order preserves rather than displaces Congress's own reserved authority over specific trust lands.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Affects a broad, undefined universe of individual and tribal land parcels nationwide in a single order; flagged for completeness, though the direction of the effect is protective (preventing loss of trust status) rather than a structural loophole, so it is not scored as a concern under this flag.

title: Executive Order 9501—Regulations Governing Recall to Active Duty of Retired Commissioned Officers of the Public Health Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific July 1944 statute (§211(d), Public Health Service Act) governing recall-to-duty procedures for retired PHS officers; a bounded, largely mechanical delegation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective procedure (medical examination, defined recall categories), with a genuine, disclosed outer bound on wartime age-based recalls: 'not... extend beyond the last day of the sixth month following the termination of the state of war.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9502—Establishing Special Temporary Positions in the Public Health Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes two named temporary PHS positions under a specific July 1944 statute; narrow, bounded personnel action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope (two positions, defined grade).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed dual-trigger sunset: the positions 'cease to exist upon the termination of the present war or of the emergency declared by me on May 27, 1941, whichever is earlier' — an objective, externally-anchored termination condition (the EARLIER of two disclosed triggers), a real if not date-certain sunset.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9504—Revoking the Designation of Dunkirk, New York, as a Customs Port of Entry

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow revocation of a single port-of-entry designation; administratively contracts rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named port, fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9503—Appointment of Disabled Veterans Completing Courses of Instruction Prescribed Pursuant to the Act of March 24, 1943

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A defined, statute-anchored appointment-eligibility mechanism for disabled veterans completing prescribed training; not a discretionary power grant, and expressly conditioned on multiple named safeguards.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Multiple genuine, disclosed checks stacked in sequence: employing-agency recommendation required, CSC determination that the training was 'adequate,' AND a CSC-prescribed noncompetitive examination the veteran must pass — a real, disclosed multi-step gate, not a nominal one.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The multi-step CSC gate is a genuine, disclosed check, not a removed one.

title: Executive Order 9506—Modifying the Conditions Upon Which a Classified Status May Be Granted

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Tightens (rather than loosens) the procedural requirement for acquiring classified civil-service status, requiring actual selection from and entry on duty pursuant to a CSC certificate — narrows discretion rather than concentrating it.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective procedural requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order strengthens rather than removes a procedural safeguard.

title: Executive Order 9507—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment revoking specific obsolete fee-tariff line items (relating to now-defunct Chinese extraterritorial consular certificates) and two specific prior sections/orders; administratively contracts rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, itemized revocations; an express proviso preserves the non-revival of a still-earlier superseded order (EO 7224-A), a genuine disclosed safeguard against unintended reactivation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9509—Regulations With Respect to the Uniforms of Commissioned Officers of the Public Health Service, and Governing the Wearing of Such Uniforms

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extremely detailed but purely administrative uniform-specification regulation implementing a July 1944 statute; no discretionary power beyond routine dress-code administration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, highly specific criteria (measurements, materials, insignia); Surgeon General discretion is confined to scheduling/logistics of uniform-wearing occasions, not substantive policy.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9510—Transfer of Certain Personnel from the Coast and Geodetic Survey to the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized transfer of eight named commissioned officers to War Department jurisdiction while retaining their Coast and Geodetic Survey commissions; the same bounded, named-personnel template as EO 9468 earlier in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed default-return trigger: Sec. 3 requires return to the Survey 'when the present national emergency ceases to exist' — a real, if not date-certain, disclosed reversion default, and the affected officers are individually named rather than an open class.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The default-return provision is a genuine, disclosed limit, not one removed.

title: Executive Order 9512—Providing for Coordination of the Allocations of Field Positions Subject to the Classification Act of 1923, as Amended

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Directs the Civil Service Commission to prepare and publish uniform position-allocation standards which agency heads 'shall comply with' — this constrains, rather than concentrates, individual agency discretion over pay-grade classification, and vests the coordinating role in CSC (an independent, multi-member body) rather than a single executive officer.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed oversight mechanism: CSC is directed to 'ascertain by investigation' agency compliance and may report noncompliance 'to the President through the Liaison Officer for Personnel Management' — a real, disclosed monitoring-and-escalation channel.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, a disclosed compliance-monitoring mechanism.

title: Executive Order 9513—Extending the Periods of Service or Training and Service of Persons Inducted into or Assigned to the Navy, Marine Corps, or Coast Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A blanket wartime service-extension applicable to all Selective Training and Service Act inductees assigned to the naval services; broad in reach but narrow in kind (duration only), and grounded directly in a specific 1941 statute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed dual-path termination trigger distinguishing this order favorably from the pure zombie-emergency pattern seen elsewhere in this batch (EO 9441, EO 9456): extension runs only until 'termination of any such war, as declared by proclamation of the President OR by concurrent resolution of the Congress' — a real, independent CONGRESSIONAL path to termination exists alongside the presidential one, not merely an indefinitely-renewable executive proclamation.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The dual presidential-proclamation/congressional-concurrent-resolution trigger, plus the disclosed 'six months immediately following' outer window, constitutes a genuine, if deferred, sunset structure rather than an open-ended one.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the congressional-resolution path is itself a disclosed check.

title: Executive Order 9514—Amending Executive Order 9452 Authorizing the Secretary of State to Prescribe Regulations and Issue Orders and Instructions Relating to the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware, PROTECTIVE amendment to the already-scored EO 9452 (this batch, coded above): it narrows rather than expands the carve-out from the Secretary of State's delegated regulatory authority, clarifying that the exception applies only where a law OTHER than section 1752 of the Revised Statutes specifically authorizes presidential regulation of a subject — a technical tightening, not an expansion, of the underlying delegation's boundary.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Federal Register publication requirement and particular-subject carve-out from EO 9452 remain undisturbed by this amendment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No additional discretion is created beyond what was already scored in EO 9452; if anything the carve-out's boundary is clarified.

title: Executive Order 9515—Amendment of Rule 54 of Executive Order No. 4314 of September 25, 1925, Establishing Rules Governing Navigation of the Panama Canal and Adjacent Waters

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, purely technical vessel-speed-limit table amendment for Canal Zone waters; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed numeric speed limits by zone.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9516—Possession and Operation of the Transportation System of the Bingham and Garfield Railway Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A single-railroad seizure grounded in the same 1916 Army Appropriation Act rail-seizure authority used for the nationwide EO 9412 railroad nationalization (outside this batch's coding window) but applied here to one short-line carrier; the Secretary of War is granted open-ended operate-'in such manner as he may deem needful or desirable' authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies both trigger and, per the order's single operative sentence, termination — nominal, self-graded check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named railway company; narrow scope, unlike EO 9412's nationwide trigger.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he may deem needful or desirable for the effective prosecution of the war' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Termination 'within sixty days after he determines that such possession and control are no longer needful or desirable' — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Unlike EO 9412's Section 8, this order contains NO creditor/judicial-remedy bar language; a narrower, single-carrier application of the same statutory rail-seizure authority without the judicial-preemption escalation.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A single short-line carrier; modest downstream exposure relative to EO 9412's nationwide scope.

title: Executive Order 9520—Designating the Vice Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A single, fixed-term personnel designation under an existing statutory framework (Federal Reserve Act §10); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed four-year term tied to the underlying Board membership.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9518—Designating the Honorable A. R. de Jesus as Acting Judge of the District Court of the United States for Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized, calendar-year judicial-designation order for absence/illness/disability coverage; narrow statutory designation authority only, the same template as EO 9461-A earlier in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and named individual.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9519—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to the Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion, the Office of Contract Settlement, the Surplus Property Board, and the Retraining and Reemployment Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an already-existing contracting-authority delegation (EO 9001, outside this batch's coding window) to four named agencies newly created or reorganized by the War Mobilization and Reconversion Act of 1944, 'subject to the limitations and regulations contained in such Executive order' — a bounded extension of an existing mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Expressly bounded by EO 9001's own limitations, which are preserved rather than superseded.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the extension across four distinct newly-formed agencies in a single order; a modest administrative bundling, not a novel substantive grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: EO 9001's own limitations are expressly preserved, not removed.

title: Executive Order 9517—Returning Certain Land to the Jurisdiction of the Territory of Hawaii

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Returns a specific, surveyed 175,000-sq-ft tract to Territorial jurisdiction because a planned USO clubhouse was never built and the Territory requested the land back; narrows federal control rather than expanding it.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed rationale, surveyed metes-and-bounds description, and the Territory's own expressed request as the operative trigger.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes territorial jurisdiction.

title: Executive Order 9521—Revocation of Certain Executive Orders Prescribing Regulations Relating to the Duties of Officers and Employees of the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Revokes roughly 44 discrete prior Executive Orders spanning 1896-1944 governing Foreign Service duties, consolidating that regulatory authority into the Secretary of State's delegated rulemaking power under the already-scored EO 9452/EO 9514 (this batch); a large-scale consolidation of dispersed historical regulatory instruments into a single delegated authority, even though the delegation itself was already scored.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed procedural safeguards accompany the consolidation: the Secretary of State must affirmatively 'designate, in each such order or regulation... the Executive order, or part thereof... which such order or regulation is designed and intended to supersede,' publish it in the Federal Register, AND the revocation of any listed order expressly 'shall not constitute a revival' of any order that listed order had itself superseded — a carefully disclosed, non-trivial set of process safeguards against silent gaps or accidental revivals.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the revocation of 44 separately-issued historical orders (spanning nearly 50 years of Foreign Service regulation) into a single consolidating order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Federal Register publication and non-revival safeguards are genuine, disclosed limits on the consolidation, not oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9522—Transferring the Tomah Radio School Property at Tomah, Wisconsin, to the Control and Jurisdiction of the Veterans' Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-property jurisdictional transfer (378 acres) between two federal bodies under a specific 1925 statute; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed property description and statutory basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9524—Amending Executive Order No. 9195 of July 7, 1942, Prescribing Regulations Relating to Aerial Flights by Personnel of the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, and National Guard

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A detailed but purely administrative amendment extending an already-existing flight-pay regulatory framework (EO 9195, outside this batch's coding window, as amended by EO 9458 within this batch) to a newly-eligible class (Public Health Service officers detailed to the Coast Guard); no new discretionary power beyond the pre-existing framework's own terms.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed reporting chains to named service officials, consistent with the underlying framework.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9527—Relinquishing Possession of the Plants of York Safe and Lock Company, York County, Pennsylvania

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A termination/relinquishment order ending government possession taken under the already-scored EO 9416 (outside this batch's coding window); narrows rather than expands authority, and discloses that PART of the original seizure had already been separately terminated in June 1944 following condemnation proceedings — a genuine, disclosed partial-wind-down history.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: relinquishment rests on the President's own affirmative determination that the remaining plants 'will be operated privately in a manner consistent with the war effort,' per EO 9416's own 60-day post-determination termination structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes private control.

title: Executive Order 9526—Amending Certain Executive and Public Land Orders Withdrawing Public Lands for Purposes Incident to the National Emergency and the Prosecution of the War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Amends 54 prior land-withdrawal orders (52 Executive Orders and 59 Public Land Orders reserving over 13,000,000 acres) to ADD a uniform, disclosed reversion clause; this is a rare instance of an order affirmatively strengthening rather than weakening the sunset/accountability structure of a large body of predecessor orders.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed protective structure: the amendment restores 'jurisdiction over, interests in, needs and uses for, and administration of' the withdrawn lands to the departments/agencies that held them before the wartime withdrawal, once the reversion trigger fires.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Amends 54 separately-issued prior orders in a single consolidating action — a large bundling event, though notably in the ACCOUNTABILITY-STRENGTHENING rather than power-concentrating direction, which tempers but does not eliminate the structural bundling concern this flag tracks.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This order adds a genuine, disclosed, externally-anchored sunset that many of the 54 amended orders previously lacked: 'jurisdiction granted by this order shall cease at the expiration of the six months' period following the termination of the unlimited national emergency declared by Proclamation No. 2487 of May 27, 1941' — an affirmative sunset ADDITION, the inverse of the zombie-emergency pattern flagged elsewhere in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes agency jurisdiction/oversight over the affected lands.

title: Executive Order 9525—Amendment of Executive Order 9356 of June 24, 1943, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-subsection clothing-replacement allowance addition (for personnel returned from overseas as patients) to an already-existing schedule; purely arithmetic/administrative, the fourth such amendment to EO 9356 across this batch (following EO 9447, EO 9465).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figures and a genuine anti-double-recovery safeguard (later claims are offset by the value of clothing already issued).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the anti-double-recovery offset is itself a disclosed safeguard.

title: Executive Order 9528—Amending Executive Order No. 9096 to Provide a Change in the Order of Succession of Officers to Act as Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment to an internal departmental succession order (already outside this batch's coding window); purely organizational.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, enumerated succession sequence.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9529—Reinstating Paul H. Alling in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized, named-person reinstatement order, effective only upon Senate confirmation of a specific nomination — an unusually strong disclosed check (Senate confirmation as the operative trigger) for an individualized personnel action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: reinstatement is expressly contingent on Senate confirmation of the nomination, not on the President's own determination alone.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Senate-confirmation contingency is a genuine, disclosed check, not one removed.

title: Executive Order 9530—Exemption of Harry F. Hawley From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized age-retirement exemption for one named Foreign Service Officer; narrow, non-discretionary beyond the single case.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed rationale ('public interest requires') and named individual, with a genuine disclosed outer bound: exemption runs 'not to exceed the duration of the present emergency.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9531—Making Certain Changes in the Customs Field Organization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine customs-district boundary/consolidation changes (abolishing Omaha's separate district, folding it into Chicago's district); administrative reorganization, no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, enumerated jurisdictional changes with a fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9533—Changing the Name of the Customs Port of Entry of Marshfield, Oregon, to Coos Bay, Oregon

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure renaming of an existing customs port with no substantive change to jurisdiction or authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, prior orders' substantive terms expressly preserved under the new name.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9532—Changing the Name of the Office of the Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Primarily a renaming action (Coordinator of Inter-American Affairs to Office of Inter-American Affairs) plus a contemporaneous individual appointment (Wallace K. Harrison as Director); the Director's duties are the same ones 'now discharged and performed by the Coordinator,' a continuity of an already-existing office rather than a new grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check preserved: the Director acts 'under the direction and supervision of the President and in conformity with the foreign policy of the United States as defined by the Secretary of State' — real, disclosed subordination to both the President and State Department foreign-policy guidance, not merely nominal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The State Department foreign-policy-conformity requirement is a genuine, disclosed check, not one removed.

title: Executive Order 9534—Designating the Federal Loan Administrator and the War Food Administrator as Members of the Economic Stabilization Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: ADDS two named officials as members of the already-established (and previously scored, outside this batch's window) Economic Stabilization Board — broadens rather than concentrates the Board's composition, a structurally protective direction similar in spirit to EO 9535 below.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, expands a multi-member deliberative body rather than vesting new authority in a single official.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order broadens rather than narrows the Board's composition/oversight base.

title: Executive Order 9535—Public Members of the National War Labor Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Increases the National War Labor Board's public membership from four to eight (with a disclosed voting cap of four per matter) and revokes two narrower associate/alternate-member mechanisms in favor of full membership status; broadens rather than concentrates the Board's composition.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed numeric composition and voting-cap safeguard ('not more than four... shall vote on any one matter'), a genuine, disclosed anti-packing safeguard accompanying the membership expansion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order broadens the Board's public-member base with a disclosed voting-cap safeguard, rather than narrowing oversight.

title: Executive Order 9536—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and to Operate Certain Coal Mines

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A qualitatively different seizure design from every other coal order in this batch (EO 9469, 9478, 9481, 9482, 9483): rather than naming specific mines after individualized investigation, this order authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to seize 'any and all such mines' producing bituminous coal nationwide that are experiencing 'interruptions or threatened interruptions... as a result of existing or threatened strikes and other labor disturbances' — a standing, prospective, self-executing authorization applicable to any qualifying mine in the country without a further case-specific presidential finding, functionally converting the seizure power from a series of individualized determinations into an open-ended nationwide grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Some genuine disclosed constraint exists: Sec. 3 subjects any wage-and-hour changes the Secretary might negotiate to 'the national wage and price stabilization policies as determined by the National War Labor Board and the Economic Stabilization Director' — a real, disclosed external check specifically on the wage dimension, better than the peer coal-seizure orders' silence on this point. But the underlying seizure/operate determination itself remains entirely self-graded by the Secretary, with no external board reviewing which mines are actually seized under this open-ended authorization.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Unlike every other seizure order in this batch (which bundle a finite, named list of companies/mines/facilities), this order's target class is textually unbounded — 'any and all such mines' producing bituminous coal nationwide — the broadest possible bundling scope encountered in the stream to date, exceeding even EO 9466's ~124-company list and EO 9462's 103-carrier list, both of which at least named their targets.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he may deem necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' is open-ended, now applied to an unbounded target class.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 7's termination trigger — possession ends 'as soon as he determines... no longer required... but in no event more than sixty days after' restoration — remains self-graded and, layered atop the order's standing/anticipatory design, means the Secretary can seize AND release mines repeatedly and indefinitely under a single order without any further presidential re-authorization.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed date; the standing/anticipatory design compounds the usual self-graded-termination sunset weakness by removing even the requirement of a fresh presidential finding per mine.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No creditor/judicial-remedy bar comparable to EO 9412/EO 9462 is present in this order's text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The most structurally significant escalation in this batch's coal-seizure sequence: converting from case-by-case seizure orders (this batch's EO 9469, 9478, 9481, 9482, 9483) to a single standing nationwide authorization is a substantial expansion of executive seizure-power architecture, worth flagging prominently as a new pattern for the record.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 5: 'upon the request of the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of War shall take such action... as he may deem necessary or desirable to provide protection' — an open-ended cross-agency assistance clause, the same structural family as EO 9438/9462 earlier in this batch.

title: Executive Order 9537—Amending Executive Order 8396 of April 18, 1940, Prescribing Chapter I of the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment authorizing the Secretary of State to appoint (or delegate appointment of) Foreign Service clerks/employees; a routine internal personnel-authority delegation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, standard Cabinet-level personnel delegation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.
